- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Status dot lifecycle color journey — three inputs (PR > fab > tmux), hue=phase / shape=status.** Redesigned the unified `StatusDot` (`status-dot.tsx`) from `260615-yg7f`'s two-input PR-or-activity model into a three-input lifecycle journey that adds the **fab pipeline** as a first-class middle input. Reworked § Status Dot (full rewrite), § PR Status (exports + dot cross-refs), § Dashboard, § Window rows (items 1+3), the `window-row.tsx`/`status-panel.tsx` file-roster + § Collapsible Panels entries. **Precedence** (`statusDotState`): PR wins when `fabChange && prNumber` (→ `{phase:"pr", shape:prShape(win)}`); else fab when `fabChange` (→ `{phase:fabPhase(fabStage), shape:fabShape(fabDisplayState)}`); else tmux (`{phase:"none", shape: active?"solid":"ring"}`). **Hue = phase** via `PHASE_HUE`, honoring the fab-kit README's 4-phase grouping (`fabPhase`: intake→intake; apply,review→execution; hydrate→completion; ship,review-pr→shipping) with the run-kit palette — intake=`text-blue-400`, execution+completion BOTH=`text-amber-400` (apply/review/hydrate render identically), shipping=`text-accent-green`, pr=`text-purple-400`, none=`text-text-secondary`. Documented as a palette mapping over the same grouping, NOT a divergence. **Shape = status** — ONE vocabulary across fab AND PR: ring (pending), solid (active/ready), failed = DASHED ring in phase hue + a small red (`bg-red-400`) center dot, done = rounded square (`rounded-[1px]`), skipped = gray hollow ring; failed+done render at 8px (`w-2 h-2`), ring/solid at ~6px. `fabShape`: pending→ring, active/ready→solid, failed→failed, done→done, skipped→skipped, unknown→solid. `prShape` reuses `prDotState`: merged→done, fail→failed, pending→ring, healthy→solid; `neutral` splits on `prState` — closed-unmerged → the gray `skipped` ring ("PR — closed", matching the spec matrix), else → solid. The `win.prState === "closed"` check lives in `prShape`'s `neutral` fall-through only; `prDotState` still returns `neutral` for closed PRs (R9 unchanged). **Red is used in exactly ONE way**: the center dot of a failed dashed ring — the old `fabDisplayState === "failed"` whole-dot red tint AND the old solid-red PR fail dot are both REMOVED. **a11y**: `role="img"`+`aria-label`+`title` from phase+status — fab uses the stage word (`SHAPE_LABEL`: "apply — active"/"review — failed"), the PR branch uses PR-native words (`PR_SHAPE_LABEL`: "PR — merged"/"PR — failing"/"PR — checks running"/"PR — open"/"PR — closed"), tmux uses bare "active"/"idle"; `dotLabel` composes it. New impl symbols in `pr-status-line.tsx`: `DotShape`, `DotPhase` (intake|execution|completion|shipping|pr|none), `StatusDotState = {phase, shape}`, `fabPhase`, `fabShape`, `prShape`, `PHASE_HUE`; in `status-dot.tsx`: `SHAPE_LABEL`, `PR_SHAPE_LABEL`, `dotLabel`. **`PR_DOT_COLOR` and `PR_DOT_LABEL` were DELETED** (zero-call-site once the dot stopped using them) — corrects the `260615-yg7f` rows' "all stay live" claim; the dot now renders from `PHASE_HUE` + the shape maps. `prDotState`/`PrDotState`/`isFailish`/`PR_STATE_COLORS`/`PR_CHECKS_COLORS`/`PR_REVIEW_COLORS`/`PrStatusLine` are UNCHANGED (dashboard PR text line + Pane-panel pr segments; `prShape` reuses `prDotState`). Frontend-only; no backend/SSE/tmux change; no new color tokens beyond standard Tailwind `text-blue-400`/`text-amber-400`. Call sites unchanged (redesign is entirely inside `StatusDot` + `statusDotState`). New spec page `docs/specs/status-dot.md` + `docs/img/status-dot-matrix.svg` + a `docs/specs/index.md` row. Tests: `status-dot.test.tsx` (three-way precedence + `fabPhase`/`fabShape`/`prShape` + tmux fallback + a11y), conformed `window-row.test.tsx`/`dashboard.test.tsx`; `pr-status-line.test.tsx` unmodified. (0hsz)

## 2026-06-15
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Color storage int → string descriptor (swatch palette 6 → 10).** A color value is now a STRING descriptor (`"4"` single ANSI index / `"1+3"` two-hue blend) end-to-end. **tmux options**: `WindowInfo.Color` and `SessionInfo.Color` are `*string` (parsed from `@color` / `@session_color` via the new `validate.NormalizeColorValue`, which accepts a legacy bare int OR a descriptor and drops malformed). **settings**: `settings.ServerColors` became `map[string]string` — tolerant read (int-or-string), always writes quoted strings, no migration step; `Get/SetServerColor` now string-typed. **Shared validator**: new `validate.ValidateColorValue` (single 0–15 OR `a+b` each 0–15) + `NormalizeColorValue`, reused by the window-`/options` `@color` rule (`validateWindowOption`), `handleSessionColor`, and `handleSetServerColor` (no triplication). **Session-color path moved**: session color now flows through tmux `@session_color` → `SessionInfo.Color` → `ProjectSession.SessionColor`; `tmux.SetSessionColor`/`UnsetSessionColor` added. The `internal/config/runkit_yaml.go` `Read/WriteSessionColor`/`parseSessionColor` (`*int`) are confirmed DEAD (zero non-test callers) — left in place as deletion candidates, not migrated. All mutations stay POST (§IX); validated input before any `tmux set-option` (§I). Frontend wire types `WindowInfo.color?` / `ProjectSession.sessionColor?` → `string`. Go tests: `settings_test.go` (tolerant read / always-write-string), `validate_test.go` (`ValidateColorValue`/`NormalizeColorValue`), `settings_test.go`/`windows_test.go`/`sessions_test.go` (400s). See `ui-patterns.md` § Color Tinting for the picker palette + OKLab/WCAG contrast guardrail. (6rnr)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Web Push notifications — `rk notify` → closed-tab OS notification.** New `internal/push` package owns `~/.rk/vapid.json` (VAPID keypair, private key mode `0600`, lazy-generated via `webpush.GenerateVAPIDKeys`, never served) + `~/.rk/push-subscriptions.json` (subscription array, de-duped by `endpoint`, tolerant load) — filesystem state, no DB (§II); plus `Notify(ctx, title, body)` fanning out `{title, body, icon}` payloads via `webpush.SendNotificationWithContext` and pruning `404`/`410` dead endpoints. New dep `github.com/SherClockHolmes/webpush-go@v1.4.0`. Three routes in `api/push.go` + `router.go` (CORS unchanged `[GET, POST, OPTIONS]`): `GET /api/push/vapid-public-key` (`{"key":…}`, lazy keygen), `POST /api/push/subscribe` (store, de-dupe, 64 KiB bound, `400` on empty endpoint/bad JSON), `POST /api/notify` (`{title?, body}` → fan-out → `{sent, pruned}`, `400` on empty body). New CLI `rk notify <message> [--title]` (`cmd/rk/notify.go`, registered in `root.go`) resolves the base URL via `config.Load()` and POSTs `{title, body}` to `/api/notify` under an 8s timeout, **fail-silent** (exit 0 / no output on any error). `rk notify` line added to `writeCapabilities` under **Workflow** in `context.go`. Frontend: hand-written `public/sw.js` (`push` → `showNotification`, `notificationclick` → focus/open `/`), registered on load from `main.tsx` guarded by `'serviceWorker' in navigator`; `lib/push.ts` (`registerServiceWorker`/`enablePushSubscription`/`getPushState`, fail-silent); `use-push-subscription.ts` hook + `pushActions` Cmd+K palette block (`Notifications: Enable push` / `Notifications: Enabled ✓` — terminal-themed label, NOT a bell), threaded into `app.tsx`'s `paletteActions`; `api/client.ts` `getVapidPublicKey`/`subscribePush`. Requires a secure context (HTTPS or `localhost`) for SW + `PushManager`. Constitution: §II (JSON under `~/.rk/`, no DB), §IX (mutations POST), §IV (no new route/settings page — Cmd+K only), §V (keyboard-first), §I (0600 private key, never served, request-body validation, no subprocess). (xd9r)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: PR status dot traffic-light (5-state colored indicator) (#267)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **PR status dot — 5-state traffic-light in the sidebar window row.** Reworked § Window rows item 3 + § PR Status. Generalized `260613-o20f`'s single red fail-only `●` into a colored dot rendered for EVERY change-bound PR window (gate UNCHANGED: `win.fabChange && win.prNumber`). New exports in `pr-status-line.tsx` (colocated with `isFailish`, which the `fail` branch reuses — single source of truth preserved): `PrDotState`, `prDotState(win)` (first-match-wins `merged → fail → pending → healthy → neutral`), `PR_DOT_COLOR` (`merged`→`text-purple-400`, `fail`→`text-red-400`, `pending`→`text-yellow-400`, `healthy`→`text-accent-green`, `neutral`→`text-text-secondary` — no new hex), `PR_DOT_LABEL` (per-state `aria-label`+`title`; the fail label CHANGED to "PR needs attention — checks failing or changes requested"). Load-bearing reframing: **green = HEALTH, not merge-readiness** — `healthy` is purely `prChecks === "pass"` with no `&& approved`/`&& !draft`, so a draft with passing checks reads green. Four live states keep the solid `●` glyph; `neutral` renders a dim hollow ring (activity-dot `border: 1.5px solid currentColor` + transparent, `w-1.5 h-1.5 rounded-full`) so "has a PR, no news" ≠ "no PR". `merged` is the only short-circuit (transient, Constitution II — ages out to `neutral`, never persisted); `closed`-unmerged falls through to `neutral`, and a closed PR with failing checks reads `fail`. Known a11y nuance: the state-agnostic neutral label means a closed PR announces "PR open" (faithful to the Certain-grade spec; a follow-up could derive it from `win.prState`). Cross-surface: `status-panel.tsx` `getPrSegments` dropped the `win.prIsDraft ? "text-text-secondary"` draft override so a draft's state segment follows `PR_STATE_COLORS["open"]` (green) — all three PR surfaces (sidebar dot, status-panel segments, `PrStatusLine`'s shared `isFailish` color) tell ONE color story (`PrStatusLine` itself keeps its binary red/secondary line color). Frontend-only — no backend/SSE/`gh` change (reuses the six `WindowInfo` PR fields). Tests rewritten into per-state assertions: `window-row.test.tsx`, `pr-status-line.test.tsx` (`prDotState` precedence), `status-panel.test.tsx` (draft → green). (2olv)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Swatch palette expanded 6 → 10 (two-hue blends) + WCAG contrast guardrail.** Reworked § Color Tinting. A color value is now a STRING descriptor (`"4"` single ANSI index / `"1+3"` 50/50 blend, theme-derived via `blendHex`), not a bare integer. `themes.ts`: `PICKER_ANSI_INDICES` cut to `[1,2,3,4,5,6]` and superseded by `PICKER_BLEND_PAIRS` (`[[1,3],[1,4],[3,4],[1,2]]` = orange/purple/slate/olive) + `PICKER_COLOR_VALUES` (10 ordered string values); new `PickerColor` type and `parseColorValue`/`formatColorValue`/`colorValueToHex` helpers; brights 9–14 REJECTED (audit: collapse onto 1–6 on most themes). `computeRowTints` now returns `Map<string, RowTint>` (string-keyed, was `Map<number, RowTint>`); tint pipeline unchanged (saturate ×1.5 → blend 0.14/0.22/0.32, gray sentinel `"8"` at 0.5). New `computeRowBorders(palette, category) → Map<string, string>`: each color's 8px left border passes through `adjustBorderForContrast` (nudge ONLY OKLab L until WCAG ≥ `BORDER_MIN_CONTRAST` 3.0, preserve hue/chroma, 24-step cap) — a legibility fix that also shifts SOME existing-6 borders on ~25/70 themes. New ported OKLab/WCAG helpers (`hexToOklab`/`oklabToHex`/`relativeLuminance`/`contrastRatio`/`adjustBorderForContrast`, from the dev-time audit script `scripts/audit-swatch-colors.ts`). `swatch-popover.tsx`: 10-swatch grid, string `selectedColor?`/`onSelect` props, 11-item keyboard nav reaching every blend + Clear. `sidebar/index.tsx` memoizes both `rowTints` + `rowBorders` and threads to `window-row.tsx`/`server-panel.tsx` (border looked up by color value). Wire types `WindowInfo.color?: string` / `ProjectSession.sessionColor?: string` (`types.ts`); `client.ts` color fns string-typed. Tests: `themes.test.ts` (parse/format, string-keyed tints, OKLab round-trip, contrast bounds, adjust clears/preserves/passes-through), `swatch-popover.test.tsx`, `client.test.ts`. No E2E (no spec asserted swatch behavior; covered by Vitest). (6rnr)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Web Push opt-in via Cmd+K (terminal-themed, not a bell).** New § Notifications (Web Push opt-in): the push opt-in is a single command-palette action whose label reflects state — `Notifications: Enable push` (runs `Notification.requestPermission()` → `getVapidPublicKey()` → `pushManager.subscribe({userVisibleOnly: true, applicationServerKey})` → `subscribePush()`) vs `Notifications: Enabled ✓` (Unicode checkmark, terminal-themed glyph — a bell was explicitly rejected). Satisfies §V (keyboard-first) + §IV (no new route/settings page). `usePushSubscription()` hook (`hooks/use-push-subscription.ts`) returns `{ state, actions }`; the `pushActions` block is spread into `app.tsx`'s `paletteActions` just before `windowSwitchActions`. Flow logic in the self-contained `lib/push.ts` (`registerServiceWorker`/`enablePushSubscription`/`getPushState`) + `api/client.ts` (`getVapidPublicKey`/`subscribePush`); `public/sw.js` registered on app load from `main.tsx`. All paths throw-free: registration guarded by `'serviceWorker' in navigator`, enable flow aborts silently on denied/unsupported/insecure context. Requires a secure context (HTTPS or `localhost`) for SW + `PushManager`. Backend/delivery model in `architecture.md` § Web Push Notifications. (xd9r)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Unified status dot — one `StatusDot` subsuming activity + PR, reused on three surfaces.** New § Status Dot + reworked § PR Status, § Dashboard window cards, § Window rows (items 1+3), and the `window-row.tsx`/`status-panel.tsx` file-roster entries. New `app/frontend/src/components/status-dot.tsx` — `StatusDot({ win })` renders ONE dot per window via a new `statusDotState(win)` helper + `StatusDotState` discriminated union (`{ kind: "pr"; pr: PrDotState } | { kind: "activity"; active: boolean }`), both colocated with `prDotState`/`PR_DOT_COLOR`/`isFailish` in `pr-status-line.tsx`. **Precedence**: PR status wins when `win.fabChange && win.prNumber` (→ `prDotState`: merged/fail/pending/healthy/neutral); else falls back to activity (`win.activity === "active"`). **Visual vocabulary — all color = PR meaning, activity = shape-only**: PR states render as the prior sidebar PR dot (solid `●` in `PR_DOT_COLOR`; `neutral` = dim hollow ring); the activity fallback is MONOCHROME fill-vs-ring (active = gray `text-text-secondary` filled, idle = gray hollow ring) — NO green, so green/purple/red/yellow are never ambiguous. The `fabDisplayState === "failed"` red tint is preserved on the ACTIVITY branch ONLY (a PR-branch dot keeps its `PR_DOT_COLOR`). Every state carries `aria-label` + `title` (PR reuses `PR_DOT_LABEL`; activity uses "active"/"idle"). Three call sites collapse onto it: sidebar `window-row.tsx` — the former leading activity dot AND the trailing right-side PR dot become ONE leading `<StatusDot>` (so a change-bound-with-PR window now shows its PR status, incl. purple "merged", in the high-attention LEADING position; non-PR windows show monochrome activity there); dashboard `dashboard.tsx` — the inline green/gray activity dot is replaced by `<StatusDot>` and the "active"/"idle" word is DROPPED (idle duration kept), so the dashboard card now matches the sidebar byte-for-byte and gains PR status; pane panel `status-panel.tsx` — a `<StatusDot>` is added to `WindowPanel` `headerRight` before `{win.name}` (an at-a-glance summary that survives panel collapse; the `pr`/`run` detail rows are untouched). Builds on #267 (sidebar PR dot) and #268 (shared PR color vocabulary); no new color tokens; frontend-only (all fields already on `WindowInfo` via SSE). No now-dead exports — `prDotState`/`PR_DOT_COLOR`/`PR_DOT_LABEL` all stay live. Tests: new `status-dot.test.tsx` (precedence + all 5 PR states + activity active/idle + fab-failed red-on-activity-only + per-state a11y); conformed `window-row.test.tsx` (single-dot structure), `dashboard.test.tsx` (asserts dot aria-label not the dropped word), `pr-status-line.test.tsx`. (yg7f)

- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Mobile drawer accessibility — focus trap + Escape + current-row focus.** Reworked § Sidebar → Mobile: the overlay's `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"` contract is now honored. New shared `useFocusTrap(containerRef, active, onEscape)` hook (`app/frontend/src/hooks/use-focus-trap.ts`, extracted from the `dialog.tsx`/`command-palette.tsx` focus-cycle; adopted by the drawer only — refactoring those modals is a follow-up Non-Goal). `Shell` attaches a `drawerRef` to the `<aside>` and drives the hook with `active = isMobile && sidebarOpen && !!sidebarChildren` (desktop sidebar is a grid region, NEVER trapped), wiring Escape → `setSidebarOpen(false)` (additive to backdrop/destination/hamburger dismissals). On activation focuses the first focusable; Tab/Shift+Tab wrap within the `<aside>` (`preventDefault` only at boundaries); listener attaches on `document` only while active; `onEscapeRef` stable-ref; no focus-return on close (matches `Dialog`/`CommandPalette`). R10 nested-modal deference: `hasNestedDialog(container)` detects a `[role="dialog"][aria-modal="true"]` DESCENDANT (`KillDialog`→`Dialog`) and the trap's `handleKeyDown` early-returns before Escape AND Tab, so one Escape dismisses only the topmost modal and the drawer-wide Tab wrap can't pull focus out of it; the `<aside>`'s own `role="dialog"` is excluded (`querySelector` descendants-only + `!== container` guard). The `aria-modal="true"` qualifier is load-bearing (PR review): non-modal `PinPopover` (`role="dialog"`, no `aria-modal`, no own trap) must NOT disable the drawer wrap. Capture-phase `stopPropagation` listeners (`PinPopover`/`SwatchPopover`) suppress the bubble-phase trap Escape, so no collision there. Bonus (§ Window rows): `Sidebar` attaches `navRef` to `<nav aria-label="Sessions">`; on mobile drawer open a `useEffect` queries `[data-window-id] [aria-current="page"]`, `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` + `focus()`es it (deferred via `requestAnimationFrame` to supersede the trap's first-focus), scoped to WINDOW rows so the active `BoardsSection` row (also `aria-current="page"`, no `[data-window-id]` ancestor) and board routes no-op. Frontend-only; no new dependencies. Tests: `use-focus-trap.test.tsx`, extended `shell.test.tsx`, `sidebar/index.test.tsx` current-row case. (o20f)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **User-controllable terminal font size (global, frontend-only).** Reworked § Terminal Font Scaling → § Terminal Font Size: `ChromeContext` owns a global terminal-font preference stored as `number | null` (null = unset) in `localStorage["runkit-terminal-font-size"]`, exposing the **effective** size `terminalFontSize: number` = preference ?? device default (11px mobile / 13px desktop via the shared `isMobileViewport()` narrow-width-OR-coarse-pointer rule, replacing the old `matchMedia('(min-width: 640px)')` read). Bounds 8–24px / step 1 exported as `TERMINAL_FONT_BOUNDS`; helpers `clampTerminalFont`/`deviceDefaultFontSize`/`readTerminalFontSize` mirror the `fixedWidth`/`SIDEBAR_WIDTH_BOUNDS` shape. Mutators `increaseTerminalFont`/`decreaseTerminalFont` (clamp+persist, try/catch noop) and `resetTerminalFont` = forget preference (`removeItem` → device default, not a fixed value); first step from `null` operates on the device default and persists an adjacent value (desktop 13 → 14). `TerminalClient` reads the effective size for the xterm `fontSize` option + `document.fonts.load` preloads (replacing `isMobile ? 11 : 13`), and an effect keyed on it sets `options.fontSize` + calls `fitAddon.fit()` on change (uninitialized-terminal-guarded) — reflows the PTY; the global value means every live `TerminalClient` reacts (board panes included); touch-scroll `LINE_HEIGHT` reads `options.fontSize` live. Surfaced via a top-bar `TerminalFontControl` combo `[−] {size} [+]` + reset near `FixedWidthToggle` (bounds-disabled buttons, aria-labels, `coarse:36px` sizing) AND three palette actions (`terminal-font-increase/decrease/reset`, no shortcut) wired into BOTH the AppShell palette (`app.tsx`) and the board-route palette (`board-page.tsx`). `Cmd +/-` deliberately NOT intercepted (native zoom retained). Deferred: HTML/UI-zone scaling, per-pane sizing, tmux `@rk_font_size` cross-restart persistence. Tests: `chrome-context.test.tsx` (read/clamp/increase/decrease/reset + unset→first-step), `top-bar.test.tsx` (combo render, bounds-disable, dispatch). Frontend-only — no backend change. (oo89)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Sidebar keyboard navigation + tree ARIA (Wave 3).** New § Sidebar → Keyboard Navigation & Tree ARIA: the session/window tree becomes a W3C-APG two-level disclosure tree with roving-tabindex arrow nav, scoped to the `role="tree"` container (`index.tsx`) — never `document`/terminal, no xterm conflict. ARIA: scrollable Sessions region `role="tree"` (the `<nav aria-label="Sessions">` landmark stays separate); session rows `role="treeitem"` `aria-level={1}` + `aria-expanded`/`aria-setsize`/`aria-posinset` and `aria-controls` emitted ONLY while expanded (the `role="group"` window list mounts only when `!isCollapsed`; SF-5); window rows `role="treeitem"` `aria-level={2}` leaves; window-list container `role="group"` id `windows-${server}-${session}`; server `<section>` headers stay structural wrappers (two levels). Roving nav: exactly one row `tabIndex={0}` (threaded as a single primitive so an arrow press re-renders only the two affected memo'd rows — Wave-2 memo preserved); ArrowDown/Up next/prev, stop at ends (no wrap), continuous across open groups; ArrowRight expand/first-child, ArrowLeft collapse/parent; Enter+Space activate via a typed `RowIdentity` discriminated union calling `onSelectWindow` DIRECTLY (no `.click()` synthesis; ghost/empty-windowId rows no-op — SF-2/SF-3); Home/End first/last; handler early-returns on input/editable targets so rename Enter/Escape stands. LOAD-BEARING: the cursor is a `rovingKey` STRING (`data-window-id`/`data-session-row`), and the key-normalization effect is gated on `[rovingKey, rowsVersion]` where `rowsVersion` bumps ONLY when a group's visible-row-SET signature changes (`registerGroupRows`) — NOT on passive SSE ticks; `focusMovedRef` gates the sole `row.focus()` to keypress-driven moves, so the Wave-2 #262 invariant holds (an SSE tick never changes roving state or steals focus). The mobile-drawer focus effect now `setRovingKey`s the row it focuses (SF-4, tab-stop/focus desync). DOM focus + `scrollIntoView({ block: "nearest" })` reuse the "Keyboard-Navigable List Scroll Pattern" (now a third consumer; § updated) but diverge from its `selectedIndex` numeric model. Frontend-only — `index.tsx` + `session-row.tsx` + `window-row.tsx`. Accepted caveats: inner row buttons stay natively Tab-focusable (keyboard-first); `role="tree"` wraps the server `<section>`s. Tests: `index.test.tsx` (tree ARIA + roving + SSE-tick regression), `session-row.test.tsx`/`window-row.test.tsx` (roles/level/set-pos/tabindex; `aria-controls` present-when-expanded), Playwright `e2e/sidebar-keyboard-nav.spec.ts` + `.spec.md`. (wt1v)

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Live PR status in sidebar (three network-isolated layers).** New `internal/prstatus` package: `Collector` (mirrors `internal/metrics.Collector`) holds `byNumber map[int]PRStatus` under `sync.RWMutex` + `interval`; `NewCollector`/`Start(ctx)` (warm-on-start then ticks)/`Snapshot()` (deep copy under RLock)/`RefreshNow(ctx)`/private `refresh(ctx)`. `refresh` makes ONE `gh api graphql viewer.pullRequests(first:100, states:OPEN)` call via the injectable `ghExec` seam (`exec.CommandContext` + arg slice + 10s `ghTimeout`, no shell string — §I), gated by the injectable `available` seam (`ghAvailable` = `LookPath("gh")` + `gh auth status`; absent/unauth → silent no-op). Successful refresh REPLACES `byNumber` wholesale (merged/closed PRs drop out next cycle = cleanup, no eviction logic); a `gh`/parse error keeps the last-good map (stale-while-revalidate). Pure enum-collapse helpers `mapState`/`mapChecks`/`mapReview` map GitHub's `state`+`isDraft`/`statusCheckRollup.state`/`reviewDecision` to `open|merged|closed` / `pass|fail|pending|none` / `approved|changes_requested|review_required|none`. In-memory only — no DB/disk/tmux-option (§II). **Apply-time correction**: the intake's literal `gh search prs --author @me` argv was replaced by `gh api graphql` because `gh search prs` doesn't expose the rollup/review JSON fields (errors). **Layer 1** (`internal/sessions`): `paneMapEntry` gains `PrURL`/`PrNumber` (`pr_url`/`pr_number` from `fab pane map`, released fab 2.1.2), assigned onto the window in the `enrichByWindowID` join. `tmux.WindowInfo` gains `PrURL *string`/`PrNumber *int` (Layer 1) + `PrState`/`PrChecks`/`PrReview string` + `PrIsDraft bool` (Layer 3), all with `omitempty` json tags. **Layer 3** (`api/sse.go`): new one-method `PRStatusSnapshotter` seam (`Snapshot() map[int]prstatus.PRStatus`, implemented by `*prstatus.Collector`), held on the hub via `newSSEHub(fetcher, mc, pc)`; `attachPRStatus(sess)` runs in `poll()` after the cache write as a PURE in-memory read (zero network on the 2.5s hot path), reset-first (zeroes the four display fields, safe to mutate the 500ms-cached slice) then attaches only under the change-bound gate (`FabChange != "" && PrNumber != nil`). `api/router.go` constructs/starts `pc := prstatus.NewCollector(prStatusPollInterval)` next to the metrics collector, stores it on `Server.prStatus`, and passes a true-nil interface through `initSSEHub` when unset (avoids the typed-nil trap). New const `prStatusPollInterval = 90 * time.Second` (`api/sse.go`). New `POST /api/pr-status/refresh` (`api/pr_status.go` → `handlePRStatusRefresh` → `s.prStatus.RefreshNow(ctx)` → `200 {"ok":true}`, nil-collector safe; §IX). Frontend: `WindowInfo` (`src/types.ts`) gains the six PR fields with literal-union types; `refreshPrStatus()` POST wrapper (`src/api/client.ts`); shared `PrStatusLine` component (`src/components/pr-status-line.tsx`) reused by sidebar `WindowRow` + dashboard cards; dashboard card refactored from `<button>` to `<div>` wrapper so the PR `<a>` link sits outside the button (valid HTML). Tests: `prstatus_test.go` (wholesale rebuild, stale-while-revalidate, gh-absent no-op, enum collapse), `sessions_test.go` (pane-map PR enrichment by WindowID), `pr_status_test.go`, `pr-status-line.test.tsx` + `window-row.test.tsx` (gate + link), Playwright `pr-status-sidebar.spec.ts` + sibling `.spec.md`. External runtime dep: `gh` CLI (optional — fails silently if absent/unauth; ~40 calls/hr vs. 5000/hr limit). (596o)

Renders `PR #<n> <glyph> <state>[ (draft)] · <checks/review summary>` ONLY under the change-bound gate `fabChange && prNumber` (early-returns null otherwise — the display mirror of the backend's attach gate). `PR #<n>` is an `<a href={prUrl} target="_blank">` with `onClick` stopPropagation (opens the PR, doesn't select the window); clicking the rest of the line triggers best-effort `refreshPrStatus()` (`POST /api/pr-status/refresh`, errors swallowed, refreshed status arrives via SSE). Color tokens: `text-text-secondary` default, `text-red-400` when fail-ish (`prChecks === "fail" || prReview === "changes_requested"`) — established convention, no new hex; link hover `hover:text-text-primary hover:underline`; `coarse:py-1` touch target. Dashboard window card refactored from a `<button>` root to an outer `<div>` wrapping the `<button>` so the PR `<a>` link sits OUTSIDE the button (valid HTML — no `<a>`-in-`<button>`); sidebar likewise renders the line outside the row `<button>`. `WindowInfo` (`src/types.ts`) gains six PR fields (`prUrl?`, `prNumber?`, `prState?`, `prChecks?`, `prReview?`, `prIsDraft?` with literal-union types); new `refreshPrStatus()` client wrapper. - **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Same-session relay reconnect eliminated — connection identity keyed on (server, owning session), part 2.** Reworked § Terminal Write Batching's closing paragraph: part 1's "named follow-up" is now implemented. The connect effect's deps changed from `[terminalReady, sessionName, windowId, server, wsRef]` to `[terminalReady, server, wsRef, connectionEpoch]`; same-session windowId switches ride the existing socket (the relay URL reads `windowIdRef.current`; xterm scrollback preserved — native tmux-attach behavior, since the directly-attached PTY redraws in place via the REST `selectWindow` paths already in app.tsx). New session-identity watcher on `[sessionName, server]`, declared BEFORE the connect effect (must read the pre-change server from `connectedServerRef` to skip its bump on a same-commit server+session change — the `server` dep already reconnects; bumping too would double-reconnect): `""`→resolved is absorption-only (cold deep-link — the relay already attached to the owning session server-side, no reconnect); resolved→resolved bumps `connectionEpoch` (cross-session nav, window move; session RENAME also reconnects — accepted tradeoff, no stable session id in the SSE snapshot); resolved→`""` is a LOSS-OF-IDENTITY signal that records `""` AND bumps for a probe reconnect — the window left the snapshot (killed externally, pinned to a filtered `_rk-pin-*` session, dead deep link) and the probe either re-resolves server-side (X→`""`→X ghost gap costs one flicker-free reconnect) or closes 4004 → `onSessionNotFound` redirect — the ONLY reachable recovery path in that state (`computeKillRedirect` and the URL writeback are gated on non-empty `sessionName`; review finding M1, fixed in rework cycle 1); `""`→`""` is a no-op. Part-1 deferred-reset + adaptive-flush semantics unchanged on every connection that IS established. New "TerminalClient connection identity" unit tests in `terminal-client.test.tsx`; part-1 teardown test re-triggered via a cross-session change (spec-conformant per constitution § Test Integrity). Frontend-only — `terminal-client.tsx` + its test file. (9umy)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **PR status moved to the Pane panel; removed from the sidebar tree.** Reworked § PR Status: the primary PR surface is now a copyable `pr` row in `WindowPanel` (`status-panel.tsx`), appended after `fab` in the `tmx/cwd/git/run/agt/fab` idiom — gated change-bound, copies the PR URL on click, `text-red-400` when checks fail / changes requested, Nerd Font git-pull-request icon. The Pane panel reflects the *selected* window (URL `/$server/$window`), so it's per-window detail rather than a tree-wide glance. `PrStatusLine` was REMOVED from `window-row.tsx` (the sidebar tree no longer shows PR status) but KEPT on the dashboard window cards (the dashboard route has no Pane panel). `window-row.test.tsx` updated to assert the row is absent; new `status-panel.test.tsx` `pr row` describe block (renders/gated/copies-URL/red-on-fail); `pr-status-sidebar.spec.ts` + `.spec.md` rewritten to select a window and assert the Pane panel `pr` row (mobile + desktop). Composes with the PR #241 review fixes (open-only state, no `role="button"` on the dashboard line). (obky)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Deferred terminal reset — window-switch flicker fix.** Reworked § Terminal Write Batching: now documents the adaptive flush (immediate synchronous path for ≤64-UTF-8-byte idle chunks, one per frame via `wroteImmediatelyThisFrame`; rAF coalescing under load; once-buffering-always-buffer ordering — PR #244/#245, shipped outside fab) plus the NEW per-connection deferred reset: each `connect()` arms an effect-scoped `pendingReset`, consumed exactly once by `consumePendingReset()` immediately before the connection's first chunk write (same tick on the immediate path / top of the same rAF callback on the coalesced path) so clear + repaint land in one presented frame — the old receipt-time `terminal.reset()` in `ws.onmessage` guaranteed ≥1 fully-cleared frame (the window-switch black-frame flicker) because the >64-byte first redraw chunk always coalesced to the next frame; old content now persists until the new redraw paints. Handoff semantics: empty flush neither consumes nor executes the pending reset (zero-message close-drain no-op; next `connect()` re-arms idempotently); effect cleanup neutralizes `pendingReset`/`textBuffer`/`binaryBuffers` so a dead connection's late-delivered onclose drain (which still runs before the `cancelled` check, keeping same-effect transient-drop tail drains working) can neither reset nor write stale content into a successor effect's terminal. Stale WS-effect comment (260508-hdjr ephemeral-grouped-session rationale) rewritten to the move-based direct-attach model (`260602-qn62`); same-session reconnects noted as redundant, with a named follow-up to key the effect teardown on the resolved owning session instead of `windowId`. Adaptive-flush machinery untouched (thresholds, `textByteLength`, frame guard, ordering). Reset-ordering unit tests added to `terminal-client.test.tsx`. Frontend-only. (qf25)

## 2026-06-09
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — **`ResolveWindowSession` restored to O(1) targeted lookup.** The body went back from the O(n) `list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}\t#{window_id}'` enumerate + first-match scan to a single targeted `display-message -t <windowID> -p '#{session_name}'` call via `tmuxExecServer` (mirroring `resolveWindowSessionIndex`). The enumeration scan was a #205 workaround for a session-group ambiguity (a window appeared under every group member, so `display-message -t @N` could resolve to an `rk-relay-*` ephemeral); `260602-qn62`'s move-based one-session-per-window model removed window sharing and the ephemerals, so the targeted lookup is now unambiguous — no `RelaySessionPrefix` filtering. Signature `(ctx, server, windowID) (string, error)` and the `fmt.Errorf("window %q not found", windowID)` not-found contract are unchanged: a tmux non-zero exit (missing `-t @N`) and an empty result both map to not-found, so the relay's "any error ⇒ close 4004" path is preserved. Fixes a relay-connect latency regression — the O(n) scan could approach the 5s connect-time budget on busy servers (many sessions + `_rk-pin-*` + `_rk-ctl`), spuriously closing healthy windows with 4004. The 5s relay timeout was intentionally left unchanged (the algorithm, not the duration, was the root cause). Scope was `tmux.go` + a `tmux_test.go` not-found-branch test only; `relay.go`/`windows.go` call sites untouched. See [[resolve-window-session-on-relay-connect]]. (enic)

## 2026-06-03
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — refactor: Move-Based Server-Scoped Boards (Pin Sessions) (#233)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix: Fix shll.ai help-tree publish — run-kit.json never lands (#232)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Teardown shll.ai help-tree publish CI (push → pull migration).** Removed the `Publish help tree to shll.ai` step from `.github/workflows/release.yml` (the deliberately-last step, ~95 lines incl. its leading "Placed LAST…" comment): producer CI (`rk help-dump help/run-kit.json` + `jq empty`), PR-opening logic (`publish_to_shllai()` token-clone + `gh pr create`), auto-merge wiring (`gh pr merge --auto --squash`), and all `SHLLAI_TOKEN` usage (`env:` + every `$SHLLAI_TOKEN`) deleted in one cut. shll.ai moved from a **push** model (each toolkit CLI PR'd its help JSON into shll.ai) to a **pull** model (shll.ai now runs `rk help-dump` itself on a schedule and captures the output), so the push transport was redundant and a redundant cross-repo write attack surface (dual writers to `help/run-kit.json` could race). The hidden `rk help-dump` subcommand (`cmd/rk/help_dump.go`) and its tests are **preserved unchanged** — it is now the single contract surface shll.ai pulls from. The `release` job's final step is now **Update Homebrew tap**; the GitHub release/tap path is unaffected (the removed step was last + best-effort). No code references to `secrets.SHLLAI_TOKEN` remain; deleting the orphaned GitHub repo secret is an out-of-tree operator follow-up (least-privilege). Reduces attack surface per Constitution §I. (iak3)

- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix: Guard createAnchor against resurrecting a killed tmux server (#234)

## 2026-06-02
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: Build-time help-dump — emit rk CLI help tree as help/run-kit.json (#230)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix: Prevent exit-empty tmux server death (#226)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **`exit-empty` server-death prevention (Constitution VI).** The `_rk-ctl` control-mode anchor is now created **unconditionally** on every server the tmuxctl Supervisor observes — a permanent session floor that keeps the count above zero so tmux's default `exit-empty on` can't reap the server. Previously `resolveBootstrap` created it only on servers empty at first connect, so servers with real sessions got no floor and collapsed once their last real session closed (relays-only → next disconnect → zero → whole-server death; killed live agent sessions, ≥3×). The floor is decoupled from the attach target (still prefers a real session; `firstSessionName` skips the anchor). `tmux.SetExitEmptyOff` sets `exit-empty off` server-globally, imperatively in `productionDial` before the anchor on every dial/reconnect (ordering closes the restart-window sliver); embedded conf sets it too. **Corrects the prior §VI compliance claim in this file**: tmuxctl now *does* create a session on servers with live user sessions (incl. `default`) — but it remains harmless (filtered from UIs via `ControlAnchorSessionName`, ungrouped, read-only `-r` attach), and `exit-empty off` is the deliberate §VI *prevention*, not a violation. Lifetime is explicit-kill-only (server dies only via `kill-server`/`rk reaper`; anchor-only servers persist by design; no cross-process state, §II). Test/e2e sockets still excluded by `isTmuxSocketCandidate`→`IsTestServerName` (no resurrection). (a1wo)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Build-time help-dump — emit rk CLI help tree as `help/run-kit.json`** — New hidden Cobra subcommand `help-dump` in `cmd/rk/help_dump.go` (`Hidden: true`, registered in `root.go` `init()`). `captureNode` walks `rootCmd.Commands()` recursively to full depth, dropping `completion`, `help`, and any `Hidden` node at every level via `includeInDump` (self-excludes `help-dump`); leaves serialize `"commands":[]` (non-nil slice, never `null`). Per-node `name`/`path`/`short`/`usage`/`text` from `cmd.Name()`/`CommandPath()`/`Short`/`UseLine()`/`UsageString()` (raw `text`, newlines preserved). Frozen top-level contract `{tool:"rk", version, captured_at (RFC3339 UTC), schema_version:1, root:node}`, mirroring `sahil87/shll.ai` `help/wt.json`. `version` from `displayVersion()` (ldflags, never hardcoded); `captured_at` via injectable package-level `nowUTC` var + pure `buildDump(root, version, now)` builder (test seam, no global time freeze). Writes to `[output-path]` arg via `os.WriteFile` (0o644) else stdout — pure in-process introspection, no subprocess/shell-string (§I Security First). `release.yml` gains a **Publish help tree to shll.ai** step placed LAST (after the GitHub Release + Homebrew tap, so a fatal in-repo failure can't preempt the published release): `dist/rk-linux-amd64/rk help-dump help/run-kit.json` (versioned artifact) + `jq empty` validation (in-repo, fails the job on bad JSON), then a best-effort auto-merge PR (not a direct push) into `sahil87/shll.ai` via `SHLLAI_TOKEN` mirroring the Homebrew-tap token-clone pattern — skips when unchanged, and logs `::warning::` (PR left open) rather than failing the release when shll.ai is unreachable / auto-merge is disabled. rk's slice of a 7-tool "Command reference" rollout; the shll.ai site-side consumer is a separate repo (out of scope). Tests: `cmd/rk/help_dump_test.go` (shape, filtering via synthetic + real tree, self-exclusion, full-depth recursion, leaf `[]` not `null`, version-from-`displayVersion()`, injected/parseable `captured_at`). (a36m)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Move-based server-scoped boards (pin sessions); relay ephemerals removed.** Boards switched from per-WebSocket `rk-relay-*` ephemeral isolation + `@rk_board` server-option encoding to a move-based pin-session model. A pinned window is `move-window`'d into its own single-window session `_rk-pin-<id>` (helpers `PinSessionName`/`WindowIDFromPinSession` in `tmux.go`); a board = the set of pin-sessions sharing an `@rk_board` SESSION var (server-scoped), with `@rk_home` (restore) + `@rk_board_order` (fractional `ComputeOrderKey`). `board.go` rewritten: pin-session-derived `ListBoardEntries`/`ListBoards`/`GetBoard` (no write-back), move-based `Pin`/`Unpin`/`Reorder` (Pin stamps 3 vars with `context.Background()`-rooted rollback + double-fault guard; idempotent incl. wrong-board re-stamp; Unpin restores to `@rk_home` or recreates a dead home via `rename-session`, no placeholder); kept `ComputeOrderKey`/`nextAppendKey`/validators/`BoardOption` (now the session-var key); deleted `parseBoardValue`/`serializeBoardValue`/`setBoardValue`/`ListAllBoardEntries`/`RemoveAllByWindowID`. **Relay** (`api/relay.go`) attaches the PTY directly to `ResolveWindowSession`'s result (home or `_rk-pin-*`) — removed the ephemeral allocation, `@rk_owner_pid` stamp, ephemeral select/kill; `ResolveWindowSession` no longer filters `rk-relay-*`. **Deleted**: `cmd/rk/serve_sweep.go` + its `serve.go` wiring; `RelaySessionPrefix`/`OwnerPIDOption`/`NewGroupedSession`/`SetSessionOwnerPID`/`GetSessionOwnerPID`/`ListRawSessionNames` from `tmux.go`; `TmuxOps.NewGroupedSession`/`SetSessionOwnerPID`. `parseSessions` skips `_rk-pin-*`+`_rk-ctl`, NO LONGER `rk-relay-*`. **SSE** (`api/sse.go`): dropped `detectKilledWindowIDs`/`previousWindowIDs`/window-kill `board-changed {cleanup}` diff + `RemoveAllByWindowID` dep, `broadcastBoardBootstrap`/`previousBoardJSON`/first-poll bootstrap/cached-board send; only pin/unpin/reorder `board-changed` events remain (`BoardEntriesFetcher` slimmed to one method). Accepted tradeoffs: multi-client active-window collisions on a shared home session; a pinned window disappears from its home session's sidebar until unpinned. Pins persist across rk restarts → no restore-sweep. `_rk-ctl` anchor + `exit-empty off` (`260602-a1wo`) unchanged — pin-session persistence relies on `exit-empty off`; `tmuxctl/` active-window derivation left untouched (investigate-only). Frontend `boards.ts`/`use-boards.ts` doc/contract updated to server-scoped pin-session derivation; `BoardEntry` shape + `board-pane.tsx`/`board-page.tsx` structure unchanged. (qn62)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix: Prevent exit-empty tmux server death (#226)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — **`exit-empty` server-death prevention — always-on `_rk-ctl` anchor floor + imperative `exit-empty off`.** Fixes the *other* recurring whole-server-death cause (distinct from the `260530-cf3g` `kill 0` grenade): tmux's default `exit-empty on` reaping a server the instant its session count hits zero. Root cause was `tmuxctl.resolveBootstrap` creating the `_rk-ctl` anchor **only** when a server was empty at first control-mode connect — so a server that already had real sessions got no floor, and when its last real session later closed (only `rk-relay-*` ephemerals left) the next relay disconnect drained it to zero and tmux reaped the whole server (no run-kit `kill-server`; killed live agent sessions; ≥3× — runWork/utils/kit). `resolveBootstrap` now **always** creates the anchor as a permanent session floor, decoupled from the control-mode **attach target** (still prefers the first real session; `firstSessionName` now skips `_rk-ctl` so the always-present anchor — which sorts ahead of lowercase names — isn't picked; `%session-window-changed` is global so attach-target choice is a minimal-diff preference, not a correctness need). New `tmux.SetExitEmptyOff(ctx, server)` (`set-option -g exit-empty off`, mirrors `tmuxExecServer`/`serverArgs`, ctx+`TmuxTimeout`) is called in `tmuxctl.productionDial` **BEFORE** `resolveBootstrap`/anchor creation, on the initial dial **and** every reconnect — ordering is load-bearing: it closes the reapable zero-session sliver during the close-then-reopen restart window before the floor is reinstalled. The embedded `configs/tmux/default.conf` also `set -g exit-empty off` for run-kit-created servers (belt-and-suspenders; imperative set covers hand-created/foreign servers the `-f` conf never reaches). Server-lifetime contract is now **explicit-kill-only**: a server dies only via `kill-server`/`rk reaper`; empty (anchor-only) servers persist by design (no auto-reap, no cross-process refcounting — §II). Concurrent multi-`rk serve` anchor creation stays benign via `isDuplicateSessionError` (now made actually-reachable + correct: `createAnchor` captures stderr since `cmd.Run()` doesn't populate `ExitError.Stderr`, and the classifier matches both the wrapped message and `ee.Stderr`). Test/e2e sockets are still excluded by `isTmuxSocketCandidate`→`IsTestServerName` so the always-create path can't resurrect them. The `sse.go` "real session disappeared" WARN is kept as defense-in-depth (covers external `kill-session`/OOM/shell-exit that prevention can't). (a1wo)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — **Move-based server-scoped boards (pin sessions) — relay ephemerals removed.** Boards switched from a per-WebSocket `rk-relay-*` ephemeral isolation layer + `@rk_board` server-option comma/colon encoding to a **move-based pin-session model**. New `tmux.PinSessionPrefix = "_rk-pin-"` + reversible `PinSessionName`/`WindowIDFromPinSession` helpers (strip/restore the `@`) + `ListPinSessionNames`. A pinned window is physically `move-window`'d into its own single-window session `_rk-pin-<id>`; a board = the set of pin-sessions sharing an `@rk_board` SESSION var (server-scoped — `move-window` can't cross servers), with `@rk_home` (restore target) and `@rk_board_order` (fractional `ComputeOrderKey`). `Pin` stamps the 3 vars after the move with `context.Background()`-rooted rollback on partial failure (double-fault guard: kill the pin-session only if move-back succeeded); idempotent incl. wrong-board re-stamp. `Unpin` restores to `@rk_home` (recreating a dead home via `rename-session`, no placeholder) then kills the pin-session; multi-unpin appends. `board.go` rewritten: kept `ComputeOrderKey`/`nextAppendKey`/`initialAppendKey`/validators/`BoardOption` (repurposed as the `@rk_board` session-var key); **deleted** `parseBoardValue`/`serializeBoardValue`/`setBoardValue`/`ListAllBoardEntries`/`RemoveAllByWindowID`/the server-option encoding. `ListBoardEntries`/`ListBoards`/`GetBoard` now derive from pin-sessions (no stale write-back). **Relay** (`api/relay.go`) attaches the PTY DIRECTLY to the `ResolveWindowSession` result (home or `_rk-pin-*`) — removed `newEphemeralRelayName`/`NewGroupedSession`/`SetSessionOwnerPID`/ephemeral-`SelectWindowInSession`/ephemeral-`KillSessionCtx`; `ResolveWindowSession` no longer filters `rk-relay-*`. **Deleted**: `cmd/rk/serve_sweep.go` (whole file — `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions`/`pidAlive`/`relayOwnerIsDead`) + its `serve.go` wiring; `RelaySessionPrefix`/`OwnerPIDOption`/`NewGroupedSession`/`SetSessionOwnerPID`/`GetSessionOwnerPID`/`ListRawSessionNames` from `tmux.go`. `parseSessions` now skips `_rk-pin-*` + `_rk-ctl`, NO LONGER skips `rk-relay-*`. **SSE** (`api/sse.go`): dropped the window-kill `board-changed {cleanup}` diff + `RemoveAllByWindowID` dep, `detectKilledWindowIDs`/`previousWindowIDs`, and the `broadcastBoardBootstrap`/`previousBoardJSON` first-poll bootstrap; only explicit pin/unpin/reorder `board-changed` events remain. `TmuxOps` lost `NewGroupedSession`/`SetSessionOwnerPID`. Accepted tradeoffs: multi-client active-window collisions on a shared home session; a pinned window disappears from its home session's sidebar until unpinned. Pins persist across rk restarts → NO restore-sweep. `_rk-ctl` anchor + `exit-empty off` backstop UNCHANGED (pin-session persistence relies on `exit-empty off`); `tmuxctl/` active-window derivation left untouched (investigate-only). Frontend `boards.ts`/`use-boards.ts` doc/contract updated to server-scoped pin-session derivation; response shape + component structure unchanged. (qn62)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: Fix Transient "Server not found" Flash After Server Create (#229)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix-sidebar-group-expand (mss7)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Fix non-current sidebar group expand (StrictMode purity)** — `toggleServerSection` made pure: the `localStorage.setItem` write to `runkit-panel-sessions-{server}` and the lazy `attachServer(server)` call moved OUT of the `setServerSectionsOpen` updater. Root cause: React 19 StrictMode double-invokes updaters, and the in-updater `localStorage` write was observed by the second pass (which re-read it via `readServerOpen`), inverting `next` and making a single Expand click on a non-current group a no-op (the group never opened — `multi-server-sidebar.spec.ts:70` failed deterministically). Fix snapshots `current = readServerOpen(server)` once, computes `next`, runs side-effects once outside the updater, then commits a pure functional update deriving `next` from `prev` (fallback to the `current` snapshot for untouched groups) for batch-safety. StrictMode-wrapped click-toggle regression test added in `index.test.tsx`. No backend change; existing coupling/persistence behavior preserved. (mss7)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Move-based boards — SESSIONS-vs-BOARDS exclusivity.** New § SESSIONS-vs-BOARDS exclusivity: pinning a window physically MOVES it (`tmux move-window`) into its own `_rk-pin-*` pin-session, so it disappears from its home session's window list in the SESSIONS sidebar until unpinned (which moves it back to `@rk_home`, appending at the next index). "Already true" for the sidebar with no frontend work — the SSE snapshot no longer lists the moved window under its home session (pin-sessions filtered at the `parseSessions` chokepoint). Board pane rendering is UNCHANGED: each `BoardPane` still embeds the same `TerminalClient` on `/relay/{windowId}?server=<entry.server>`, and the relay now resolves `windowId` to the pin-session server-side (transparent — `BoardEntry` shape + `board-pane.tsx`/`board-page.tsx` structure untouched). `boards.ts`/`use-boards.ts` doc comments updated from "boards are explicitly cross-server" to server-scoped derivation (pins live on one server; the board LIST is summarized across servers, so `useBoards`/`useBoardEntries` still attach all known servers for `board-changed` events). Frontend-only doc/contract change; pin icon + active-board accent affordances on SESSIONS rows are unchanged. (qn62)

## 2026-05-31
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: add --skip-brew-update flag to update command (#218)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix: Test-scoped server enumeration (#219)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — **Test-scoped server enumeration — env-gated `RK_SERVER_ALLOWLIST` allowlist in `ListServers`.** New const `tmux.ServerAllowlistEnv = "RK_SERVER_ALLOWLIST"` + pure predicate `matchesServerAllowlist(name, allowlist)` (`internal/tmux/tmux.go`). `ListServers` reads the env directly via `os.Getenv` in-package (matching the `RK_TMUX_CONF`/`OriginalTMUX` precedent — NOT via `internal/config`; `ListServers` is a `ctx`-only free fn) and, when set, narrows the post-probe live-server list to names that `HasPrefix` ANY trimmed, non-empty comma-delimited token. Empty/whitespace-only value = treated as UNSET = admits everything (production no-op, byte-for-byte). Prefix (not exact) because multi-server e2e specs create `rk-test-e2e-<role>-<pid>-<epoch>` secondaries in `beforeAll` that exact match would exclude; `rk-test-relay-*` under the broader `rk-test-` umbrella is NOT admitted. Filter lives in `ListServers` (not `handleServersList`) so ALL enumeration consumers inherit the scope — `/api/servers`, `board.go` `ListAllBoardEntries`/`GetBoard`, the `serve_sweep.go` relay-orphan sweep; the `tmuxctl` supervisor is unaffected (uses `os.ReadDir` + `isTmuxSocketCandidate`, never `ListServers`). The new forward allowlist (hides NORMAL servers from tests) is orthogonal to and does not touch the existing `IsTestServerName` denylist (hides TEST servers from normal operation). `scripts/test-e2e.sh` exports `RK_SERVER_ALLOWLIST=$E2E_TMUX_SERVER` into the `setsid … just dev` backend launch — scoping the backend READ path, distinct from `E2E_TMUX_SERVER` (the WRITE socket). Hermetic unit test `TestMatchesServerAllowlist`. **Why:** bounds board-route SSE to one `EventSource` per test server, removing the load-dependent HTTP/1.1 connection-pool starvation (N live operator servers → N EventSources → N of 6 slots) that made board e2e specs flaky only on busy boxes — the environmental third vector of the board-route hang. No prod behavior change (env unset). (tmnm)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: bound desktop board relay WebSockets on plaintext origins (#216)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: Static xterm imports (#215)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Static xterm imports** — the six xterm-family value imports in `terminal-client.tsx` (`@xterm/xterm` `Terminal`, `addon-fit`, `addon-clipboard`, `addon-web-links`, `addon-unicode-graphemes`, `addon-webgl`) converted from per-pane-mount runtime `await import()` to static top-of-file `import` statements. Because the file is already router-lazy, the xterm family now bundles into the deferred terminal-route chunk and loads **once when that chunk loads**, instead of issuing up to `6×N` runtime chunk fetches when a board mounts N panes. This removes the chunk fetches from the browser's HTTP/1.1 ~6-per-origin connection budget at terminal-init time — the confirmed root cause of the board-route E2E hang (`boards-same-session-multi-pane`, `shell-rotation`): on the plaintext dev/test origin a 7th xterm chunk fetch hung pending behind the saturated long-lived streams (Vite HMR, SSE, per-pane relay WS), so `setTerminalReady(true)` never ran and the pane stayed blank. Masked in prod over Tailscale HTTPS (h2 multiplexes). The font-load `await Promise.all([...])` is now the only async boundary in `init()`; the inter-addon `cancelled` re-checks that guarded the removed import awaits were dropped, leaving one post-construction dispose guard. WebGL `new WebglAddon()`/`loadAddon` `try/catch` retained for GPU-context runtime failures (only the module load moved to static). CSS side-effect import + type-only refs unchanged. Implementation-only — no behavior change; `terminal-client.test.tsx` already mocked all six modules, so it passed unmodified. Second drafted fix (bounding desktop board-row relay WebSockets) remains out of scope. (m3pl)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Bounded desktop relay WebSockets** — new § Desktop Relay-Connection Suspension. On plaintext origins (`window.location.protocol === "http:"`) `DesktopRow` drives each `BoardPane`'s `paused` prop from an `IntersectionObserver` rooted on `rowRef` (was hardcoded `paused={false}`), unmounting off-screen panes' `TerminalClient` so the `/relay/<wid>` WebSocket closes and the connection slot frees — the desktop analogue of the mobile carousel's `paused={idx !== carouselIndex}`. Pre-warm `RELAY_PREWARM_ROOT_MARGIN` (one pane-width horizontal `rootMargin`, no debounce) prevents scroll-past thrash. Live panes capped at `MAX_LIVE_RELAY_PANES = 4` via the pure `selectLivePanes` helper (`select-live-panes.ts`, colocated unit tests): focused pane always live (exempt from visibility-pause and the cap, preserving `Cmd+]`/`Cmd+[` cycling + BottomBar targeting), then most-recently-focused visible panes fill remaining slots, least-recently-focused paused first beyond the cap. Pane elements observed via a `data-paneIndex` + `rootRef` callback prop on `BoardPane`, distinct from the `paneRefs` imperative `BoardPaneHandle`. On HTTPS/h2 (production via Tailscale) the feature is OFF — every pane stays live, no observer, no cap — because the ~6-connection ceiling is a plaintext HTTP/1.1 artifact (h2 multiplexes; relay WS limit ~255). Composes with sibling `260531-m3pl-static-xterm-imports` (removes xterm chunk-fetch pressure): together they fit the board route under 6 connections, fixing the plaintext board-route E2E hang. `MobileCarousel` untouched. E2E `boards-desktop-suspend.spec.ts` + companion `.spec.md`. (rus8)

## 2026-05-30
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix: Active-Window Event-Driven Derivation (#212)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix: Isolate Relay Sweep & Stop Test Artifact Leaks (#208)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — refactor: Window-State API Stability Remediation (#207)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Window-state API stability remediation** — completes the index→`@N` convergence and brings the API into §IX conformance. **REST `/select` session-scoped**: `handleWindowSelect` resolves the owning session server-side (`ResolveWindowSession`) and issues `select-window -t <session>:@N` via the new `SelectWindowInSession` TmuxOps method — never a bare `select-window -t @N` (group-member disambiguation; the client never sends the session). **Single `decodeWindowID(r)` helper** (`api/windows.go`) does `url.PathUnescape` + `ValidateWindowID` in one place; `parseWindowID` and `handleRelay` both delegate to it (kills the copy-paste drift that caused #205); the misleading chi-v5 `RawPath` comment was corrected. **`MoveWindow` atomic** — resolves the source index once then emits the full bubble-swap sequence as one `\;`-chained `tmuxExecServer` invocation (no concurrent mutation can interleave mid-reorder); insert-before / sentinel / single-step / no-op semantics and the positional index destination unchanged. **`KillPane(paneID)` removed** from `tmux.go`, the `TmuxOps` interface, `prodTmuxOps`, and the mock (zero call sites; §IV); `KillActivePane`'s silent-success contract documented as the canonical pane-kill behavior. **Unified `POST /api/windows/{windowId}/options`** replaces the three separate `POST /color`, `PUT /url`, `PUT /type` routes — partial-merge body `{"options": map[string]*string}` (present key sets, `null` unsets, absent untouched), key allowlist `{@color, @rk_url, @rk_type}`, per-key validation preserved, validate-all-before-any-tmux, one atomic `\;`-chained invocation via the new `tmux.SetWindowOptions` + `appendOptionOps`/`WindowOptionOp` primitive; `handleWindowCreate`/`CreateWindowWithOptions` reuse the same primitive (no inline option-map path). **Uniform POST (§IX)** — the five `.Put(` routes migrated: `sessions/order`, `settings/theme`, `settings/server-color` (verb-only), `windows/{id}/url`+`windows/{id}/type` folded into `/options`; CORS `AllowedMethods` → `[GET, POST, OPTIONS]`; frontend client (`setWindowOptions`, `setWindowColor`/`updateWindowUrl`/`updateWindowType` delegating to it, `setSessionOrder`/`setThemePreference`/`setServerColor`) all POST. **Frontend identity on `@N` alone** — `pendingClickRef` holds `{windowId}` only and `urlMatchesPending` compares `pending.windowId === windowParam` (selection survives session rename / cross-session move). **Route shape `/$server/$window`** — `$session` dropped from the TanStack route + `parseParams`; `app.tsx` derives the session name from the active window's SSE snapshot, all `navigate` sites target the 2-segment shape, mount-time alignment + URL writeback compare/write window id only; old 3-segment URLs are a hard break (no redirect shim, constitution §II). (jad6)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Active-window event-driven derivation** — the sidebar/URL active-window highlight is now derived from tmux control-mode `%session-window-changed` events per session group, fixing the stale-base-pointer wrong-highlight bug (an `rk-relay-*` ephemeral / `rk riff` / external `new-window` moves a group member's active window without moving the base session pointer the read path used). Fix is purely in the *derivation/read path* — no window creator changed, the ephemeral relay model untouched, frontend unchanged. New `internal/tmuxctl/active_window.go` `ActiveWindowTracker` (per-server, per-Client, in-memory §II; `sync.RWMutex` over `byGroup` group→`@wid` + `sidGroup` `$sid`→group; `Set`/`Get`/`ResolveGroup`/`ReplaceSidGroups`/`SeedGroups`/`Snapshot`). `api/tmuxctl_bridge.go`: `NewHubSink()` **removed**, replaced by `NewHubSinkFactory()` returning a per-socket tracker-bound `*hubSink`; `OnSessionWindowChanged` resolves `$sid`→group (O(1) cached map, unknown-sid tolerated) and records `@wid` (latest-event-wins); `OnSessionsChanged` refreshes the map; `OnConnectionEstablished` re-seeds (map + Tier-1 from current `#{window_active}` per group, because tmux does NOT replay `%session-window-changed` on a fresh `-CC` attach) — both re-seed callbacks offloaded to a goroutine to honor the EventSink non-blocking contract; injectable `listSessionGroups`/`listActiveByGroup` query seams for testing. `internal/tmuxctl/supervisor.go`: `NewSupervisor(factory SinkFactory)` (`Supervisor.sink` field removed; `nil` factory ⇒ `NoOpSink`); one tracker per socket allocated in `openSocket`; new `Supervisor.ActiveWindow(server, group) (wid, ok)` server-keyed read accessor. New tmux helpers `ListSessionGroups`/`parseSessionGroups` (`$sid`→group, ungrouped→name fallback) and `ListActiveWindowsByGroup`/`parseActiveWindowsByGroup` (group→active-`@wid`; honors only the leader row since `list-windows -a` reports `window_active=1` per group member) — `tmuxExecServer` + `context.WithTimeout` + pure-parse-split convention (§I, §VI). Two-tier derivation in `internal/sessions/sessions.go`: `FetchSessions(ctx, server, provider)` gains an `ActiveWindowProvider` (satisfied by `Supervisor`, injected via `prodSessionFetcher` in `api/router.go` + `Server.SetActiveWindowProvider`); pure `applyActiveWindow(windows, trackedWid)` — Tier 1 (tracked `@wid` authoritative, single-highlight enforced, stale-wid falls back to Tier 2) over Tier 2 (base `#{window_active}`); nil provider degrades to Tier-2-only. Wired in `cmd/rk/serve.go`: `NewSupervisor(NewHubSinkFactory())` then `SetActiveWindowProvider(supervisor)` alongside `SetWindowChangeSubscriber`. Tests: `active_window_test.go` (`-race` concurrent read/write), `tmuxctl_bridge_test.go` (event handling + stubbed re-seed), `internal/sessions/active_window_test.go` (`applyActiveWindow` tiers + single-highlight + stale-wid), `tmux_test.go` (new parsers). Frontend verified no-change (existing `app.tsx` writeback follows once `isActiveWindow` is corrected; boards pin explicit IDs). (v6hm)

- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — feat(rk): add rk reaper command to reap leaked test tmux servers and stale sockets (#209)

- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — **Unified test-socket reaping.** All test tmux-socket names (Go + Playwright) collapsed under one umbrella `rk-test-<role>-<pid>-<ns>` (roles `unit`/`relay`/`tmuxctl`/`daemon`/`e2e`/`e2e-multi`/`e2e-coupling`/`e2e-msb`), replacing the old 5-prefix scheme (`rk-test-`, `rk-relay-test-`, `rk-verify-`, fixed `rk-tmuxctl-test`/`rk-daemon-test`) + the separate `rk-e2e` exclusion. `IsGoTestServerName` (5-prefix allowlist) **deleted** → `IsTestServerName(name) = HasPrefix("rk-test-")`, consumed only by the tmuxctl supervisor resurrection guard. `parseTestSocketPID` now parses the PID as the **second-to-last** hyphen field (was the field right after the prefix) so hyphenated roles parse; `<ns>` is a single hyphen-free token. `TestMain` sweep flipped **pre-sweep → post-sweep** (`code := m.Run(); sweepDeadTestSockets(); os.Exit(code)`) in `internal/tmux` + `api`; still PID-scoped to dead owners only (concurrent `go test` packages spared), pre-sweep dropped. Manual `rk reaper` rewritten **brute-force-by-prefix** (bare ≡ `--prefix rk-test`): no liveness probe / e2e exclusion / `.lock`-inheritance; **dry-run is the default** (both bare and `--prefix`), `--yes`/`--force` to act, only `--force` bypasses the dangerous-prefix guard (empty or ≤3 chars refused); `_rk-ctl` + live `rk-daemon` hard-skipped unconditionally. Operating contract: do NOT run `rk reaper` while tests are running (no live-run protection by design). `/api/servers` test-socket **hide filter deleted** — it now lists every server, incl. leaked `rk-test-*` orphans (accepted cost: one SSE stream per orphan until reaped). E2E (Playwright) socket names embed `process.pid` (was a `Date.now().slice(-6)` epoch); harness server is `rk-test-e2e` (was `rk-e2e`) in `scripts/test-e2e.sh`, `global-teardown.ts`, the `just pw` default, and all specs. **Separate bug fixed (constitution VI):** `scripts/test-e2e.sh` used `kill 0` in its EXIT-trap cleanup, signalling the caller's (non-detached) process group — SIGTERMing live tmux servers / `-CC` clients sharing it when run inline in an interactive/agent session (proven root cause of `kit`/`abbb`/`runWork` dying mid-session, distinct from `exit-empty`). Fixed: dev server launched via `setsid` into its own group; cleanup kills only `kill -- "-$DEV_PGID"`. (cf3g)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — Window-addressing follow-ons from the API-stability remediation. **Select scoping**: the REST `/select` handler now resolves the owning session via `ResolveWindowSession` and issues a session-scoped `SelectWindowInSession` (`select-window -t <session>:@N`) instead of the group-ambiguous bare `SelectWindow(@N)`; the relay was already scoped to its ephemeral. `SelectWindow` (bare target) retains no production callers. **Route shape**: `$session` dropped from the URL (`/$server/$session/$window` → `/$server/$window`) — `@N` is the only window identity in the URL; the owning session is derived from the SSE snapshot. **`MoveWindow` atomic**: the adjacent-`swap-window` bubble sequence now executes as one `\;`-chained invocation (source index resolved once) so no concurrent mutation can interleave mid-reorder. **Option setters**: `@color`/`@rk_type`/`@rk_url` are now set through the batched `tmux.SetWindowOptions` / `appendOptionOps` chained primitive (one atomic invocation) behind the unified `POST /api/windows/{windowId}/options`; `@rk_session_order` / theme / server-color HTTP routes migrated PUT→POST (§IX). (jad6)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — Relay startup sweep scoped to owner PID; test-artifact leaks closed. Each `rk-relay-*` ephemeral is stamped at creation with the session-scoped `@rk_owner_pid` user option (`tmux.SetSessionOwnerPID` / const `tmux.OwnerPIDOption = "@rk_owner_pid"`, mirroring `SetSessionColor`'s `set-option -t <session> @opt`; read back via `tmux.GetSessionOwnerPID` with `GetSessionOrder`-style unset/no-server tolerance). `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions` (`cmd/rk/serve_sweep.go`) now reaps a relay **only** when its owner is empty/unstamped (`relayOwnerIsDead("")→true`) or names a dead PID — `pidAlive(pid)` via `syscall.Kill(pid,0)` with a leak-not-kill bias (`nil`/`EPERM`/other→spare, `ESRCH`→reap; intentionally opposite to `daemon_portowner.go:processAlive`'s EPERM→dead). Fixes the cross-instance destruction bug where any second `rk serve` (e2e backend, `air` rebuild, manual instance) tore down a live dashboard's open terminal relays. Stamp ordering is load-bearing: after `NewGroupedSession` + cleanup-defer, before `SelectWindowInSession`/attach — an attachable-but-unstamped relay (owner=="") would be wrongly reaped; on stamp failure `handleRelay` aborts clean (slog.Warn + WS close 4001 + return; deferred `KillSessionCtx` reaps the half-owned ephemeral). Read scope is unchanged — `ListServers`/`/api/servers`/`IsGoTestServerName` still surface foreign servers (incl. `rk-e2e-*`) in the UI; only the destructive reap is scoped (single-uid socket model means EPERM is not an expected owner state). `TmuxOps` interface + `mockTmuxOps` gain `SetSessionOwnerPID`. Test-harness leaks closed too: `scripts/test-e2e.sh` trap + `global-teardown.ts` now glob all `rk-e2e*` sockets (not just literal `rk-e2e`) so timestamp-named secondaries don't leak on crash/Ctrl-C; per-package `TestMain` (`internal/tmux`, `api`) pre-sweeps `rk-test-*`/`rk-relay-test-*` sockets whose embedded PID (2nd hyphen field) is dead, self-healing SIGKILL/panic residue while sparing live-PID and fixed-name (`rk-daemon-test`/`rk-tmuxctl-test`) sockets. (wtg4)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — refactor: Window-State API Stability Remediation (#207)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **`$session` dropped from the route + identity keyed on `@N` alone.** Route shape `/$server/$session/$window` → `/$server/$window` (TanStack `terminalRoute.path: "/$window"`; `parseParams` exposes only `window`). The owning session name is now **derived from the active window's SSE snapshot** (`currentSession = sessions.find(s => s.windows.some(w => w.windowId === windowParam))`, `sessionName = currentSession?.name`) wherever it was previously read from the URL `$session` segment — breadcrumbs, dropdowns, browser title, kill-redirect inputs. `pendingClickRef` holds `{ windowId }` only (dropped `session`) and `urlMatchesPending` is `pending.windowId === windowParam` — so a session rename or cross-session move (where `@N` survives) no longer releases the pending-click suppression early and bounces the selection. Mount-time alignment guard keyed on `${server}|${windowParam}` (window-id-only); URL writeback navigates `{ to: "/$server/$window", params: { server, window } }` (no session param); `navigateToWindow(windowId)` drops the session arg. All `app.tsx` navigate sites target the 2-segment shape. Deep link `/$server/@N` derives the session server-side from the first snapshot for breadcrumb display and aligns tmux to `@N`. Old 3-segment `/$server/$session/$window` URLs are a hard break — they fall through to `NotFoundPage` / the server-dashboard fallback (no redirect shim; constitution §II). The `IframeWindow` URL bar's `updateWindowUrl` now routes through the unified `setWindowOptions` → `POST /api/windows/{windowId}/options` (`{"@rk_url": url}`) instead of the removed `PUT /url`; `setWindowColor`/`updateWindowType` likewise delegate to `/options`. E2E specs migrated to the 2-segment shape with sibling `.spec.md` updates (`sidebar-window-sync`, `multi-server-sidebar`, `mobile-touch-scroll`). (jad6)

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — refactor(windows): identify windows by stable tmux window ID (@N) instead of mutable index (#204)

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Window-ID routing (index→`@N` addressing migration)** — Window *addressing identity* migrated from the mutable tmux window index to the stable window ID (`@N`) across URL, HTTP API, WS relay, and tmux targets; the index is retained only for positional reorder/move, and sessions are still identified by name. URL: `/$server/$session/$windowId` (router percent-encodes `@`). New `validate.ValidateWindowID(id, label)` (`^@[0-9]+$`) — stricter than `ValidateName`, the security boundary for the new path param (constitution §I). HTTP: window-targeting routes moved to `/api/windows/{windowId}/...` (kill, move, move-to-session, rename, color, url PUT, **new** type PUT, keys, select, split, close-pane); create stays `POST /api/sessions/{session}/windows`. `api/windows.go` `parseWindowIndex` → `parseWindowID(r) (string, bool)` (`400` on malformed before any tmux call). `TmuxOps` + `internal/tmux` window methods take `windowID string` (no session:index target string); new `ResolveWindowSession(ctx, server, windowID)` via `display-message -p '#{session_name}'`; `MoveWindow(windowID, dstIndex, server)` resolves the source index internally then bubble-swaps; `MoveWindowToSession(windowID, dstSession, server)` runs `move-window -s <windowID> -t <dstSession>:` (tmux preserves the ID across both). WS relay route `/relay/{windowId}?server=...` resolves the owning session from the ID before creating the grouped ephemeral (malformed → `400` pre-upgrade; unknown → close `4004`); grouped-session mechanism otherwise unchanged. `internal/sessions`: pane-map enrichment + `dedupEntries` re-keyed from `session:index` to windowID (the index→ID translation built from the live `WindowInfo` snapshot, since `fab pane map` emits only session+window_index); `ProjectRoot(ctx, windowID, server)`. Frontend `api/client.ts` window-targeting fns take `windowId: string`, build `/api/windows/${encodeURIComponent(windowId)}/...`, keep `server` first; relay WS URL uses windowId. Old index-based bookmarked URLs are a **hard break** (constitution §II). Tmux-authoritative selection model (260528-nvlp) preserved — only the compared identifier changed (windowId vs `String(index)`). (chgz)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — refactor(windows): identify windows by stable tmux window ID (@N) instead of mutable index (#204)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — Window addressing migrated from mutable index to stable window ID (`@N`). New § Window Addressing Identity documents the cross-layer contract: window-targeting tmux commands pass the windowID directly as `-t` (no `session:index` string); the index is retained only for positional reorder/move; sessions are still name-identified. `internal/tmux` window methods take `windowID string` — `SelectWindow`/`RenameWindow`/`SendKeys`/`KillWindow`/`SplitWindow`/`KillActivePane`/`SetWindowColor`/`SetWindowOption` etc. New `ResolveWindowSession(ctx, server, windowID)` (`display-message -p '#{session_name}'`) and `resolveWindowSessionIndex` (adds `#{window_index}` for positional ops). `MoveWindow(windowID, dstIndex, server)` resolves source index then bubble-swaps via adjacent `swap-window`; `MoveWindowToSession(windowID, dstSession, server)` runs `move-window -s <windowID> -t <dstSession>:` — both preserve the window's `@N` (tmux contract, same as the boards pin-stability invariant). WS relay route `/relay/{windowId}` resolves the owning session from the ID before building the grouped ephemeral (malformed → 400 pre-upgrade; unknown → close 4004); grouped-session mechanism otherwise unchanged. HTTP window-targeting routes moved to `/api/windows/{windowId}/...` (create stays session-scoped); new `validate.ValidateWindowID` (`^@[0-9]+$`). `internal/sessions` pane-map enrichment + `ProjectRoot` re-keyed to windowID. Frontend `api/client.ts` window-targeting fns take `windowId` as the 2nd positional arg (server first) per the routing contract. (chgz)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — `rk reaper` operator command — a top-level sibling of `rk serve` (registered in `cmd/rk/root.go`, thin command in `cmd/rk/reaper.go`, logic in `internal/tmux/reaper.go`), NOT wired into startup and NOT replacing `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions`. Reaps three leak shapes in `/tmp/tmux-{uid}/` by iterating RAW candidates via the extracted `ScanSocketDir` (NOT `ListServers`, which drops dead sockets): (a) live orphan test servers → `KillServer`; (b) dead test sockets → `os.Remove`; (c) stale `*.lock` sockets → `os.Remove`. Pure `classifyReap(name, probeAlive) ReapAction` (`ReapActionSkip`/`Kill`/`Remove`) with branch order `.lock` → `_rk-ctl` anchor skip → `IsGoTestServerName`(+probe) → skip; `ReapTestServers(ctx, dryRun)` is the I/O entry, `reapCandidates` the temp-dir test seam. No PID-liveness gate — all five test prefixes (incl. fixed-name `rk-tmuxctl-test`/`rk-daemon-test`) and `.lock` files reap unconditionally; `rk-e2e-*` excluded for free (not in `IsGoTestServerName`), `_rk-ctl` and live non-test servers excluded explicitly. `--dry-run` previews by action and mutates nothing; default prints count/names. Per-entry `slog` log-and-skip + aggregate error mirrors the startup sweep. **Complements `260529-wtg4`'s automatic PID-gated test-socket pre-sweep (`TestMain`): that prevents new leaks during test runs; `rk reaper` is the manual, unconditional janitor for cruft already accumulated on disk.** Refactor: the socket-dir scan/probe inlined in `ListServers` was extracted into shared `socketDirPath()`/`ScanSocketDir(ctx)`/`filterSocketEntries()`/`probeServerAlive(ctx, name)` helpers (single source for the `/tmp/tmux-{uid}` convention); `ListServers` behavior preserved (probe-success-only, sorted). (fww2)
