- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Window-ID routing** — URL window segment migrated from mutable tmux window *index* to the stable window ID (`@N`): route is now `/$server/$session/$windowId` (`$session` is display-only; router percent-encodes `@` → `%402`). Mount-time alignment and URL writeback compare `activeWindow.windowId` vs the URL `@N` (was `String(index)` vs `windowIndex`) — eliminates the index-shift phantom-navigation race; `selectWindow(server, windowId)` drops the session arg. `WindowRow.isSelected` is now `hasUrlWindow ? currentWindowId === win.windowId : (!ghost && win.isActiveWindow)` (URL window ID primary when present, `isActiveWindow` fallback otherwise — inverts the prior isActiveWindow-primary/URL-fallback ordering, since the URL is now itself written back from `isActiveWindow`). Top-bar "current" detection and breadcrumb hrefs compare/build `windowId`; window/iframe-URL/split/close-pane/rename actions call client fns by `windowId` (`/api/windows/{windowId}/...`). Window-move palette actions navigate back to the same `windowId` (preserved across reorder). Nearest-window-after-kill picks a list-position neighbor and targets its `windowId` (no index-distance arithmetic). Old index-based bookmarked URLs are a hard break (no redirect). Sidebar drag-reorder optimistic action calls `moveWindow(server, srcWindowId, dstIndex)`. (chgz)

## 2026-05-28
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix(sync): event-driven active window via tmux control mode (#198)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix(daemon): port-based liveness, stale-socket reap, startup logging (#197)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **`rk daemon` CLI restructure** — Daemon lifecycle moved from flag-on-serve (`rk serve -d`/`--restart`/`--stop`) to a top-level `rk daemon` subcommand tree (sibling of `rk serve`, not nested). New files in `app/backend/cmd/rk/`: `daemon.go` (parent), `daemon_start.go`, `daemon_stop.go`, `daemon_restart.go`, `daemon_status.go`, `daemon_portowner.go` (shared `lsof`→`ss` fallback port-owner helper with `exec.CommandContext` 5s timeouts + `syscall.Kill` SIGTERM→5s poll→SIGKILL escalation, no shell strings). `--force` on `start`/`stop`/`restart` overrides the port-claim safety check by SIGTERMing a non-daemon port owner; `start --force` is lazy (only triggers on the port-in-use error path), `stop --force` probes the port after stopping, `restart --force` proactively clears non-daemon holders between stop and start. All three refuse to kill the daemon itself when the holder PID matches the new `daemon.InnerServePID()` helper (tmux `pane_pid` query via `tmux -L rk-daemon list-panes -t =rk-daemon:=serve -F '#{pane_pid}'` through the new `runTmuxOutput` capturing variant of `runTmux`). `rk daemon status [--json]` is read-only — reports daemon state (running / not running, socket, session, window, target) AND port owner (`free` / `held-by-daemon` / `held-by-other`); rejects `--force` via cobra's unknown-flag error. JSON form: `{daemon: {running, socket?, session?, window?, target?, pid?}, port: {host, port, state, holder_pid?, holder_command?}}`. Hard break on the three removed flags — no deprecation forwarders; `rk serve -d` etc. surface cobra's `unknown flag` error. `cmd/rk/serve.go` retains the `internal/daemon` import only for `daemon.LogEnvVar` (PR #197 single-source-of-truth). Naming collision with the existing `rk status` (tmux session summary) is documented in `rk daemon status --help`. `internal/daemon` public API preserved verbatim except for the additive `InnerServePID()` export; `upgrade.go`'s brew-upgrade auto-restart flow (`daemon.RestartWithBinary`) is untouched. Tests: `cmd/rk/daemon_test.go` (cobra subcommand registration, JSON shape, `--force` self-kill refusal via injected stubs, `findPortOwnerImpl` integration gated on `lsof` availability); `internal/daemon/daemon_test.go` extended with `InnerServePID` no-session and running-session cases. Constitution touchpoints: §I (all subprocess via `exec.CommandContext` + `context.WithTimeout`, signal delivery via `syscall.Kill`), §III (existing `Start`/`Stop`/`Restart`/`IsRunning` re-used unchanged), §IV (net flag-surface reduction — three flags removed, one verb with four children added). (5zvv)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Active window sync via tmux control mode** — New package `app/backend/internal/tmuxctl/` owns long-running `tmux -CC -L <socket> attach-session -t =<bootstrap> -r` subscriptions, one Client per tmux server. `Client.Open/Close/Generation/Wait` plus pure `ParseLine` for control-mode notifications (`%session-window-changed`, `%window-add`, `%window-close`, `%window-renamed`, `%sessions-changed`, `%layout-change`) and framing markers. Reconnect FSM with 250ms → 500ms → 1s → 2s → 5s capped backoff and reset on first non-`%begin` event after reconnect. `Supervisor` owns `map[socket]*Client` guarded by `sync.Mutex`, fsnotify-watches `$TMUX_TMPDIR` or `/tmp/tmux-<euid>/` (created with `os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o700)` if missing), and Open/Close-on-create/remove. Hidden `_rk-ctl` anchor session is created via `tmux new-session -d -s _rk-ctl` when a tmux server has zero existing sessions (`tmux -CC` requires an attached session); tagged with server-scoped user-option `@rk_ctl_keepalive 1` (defensive marker, no v1 consumer). New exported `tmux.ControlAnchorSessionName = "_rk-ctl"` filters the anchor from `parseSessions` (single chokepoint mirroring the `rk-relay-*` skip) and from `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions` (defense-in-depth guard). SSE loop in `api/sse.go` refactored to be event-driven via `select` over per-server `subscriber.Wait(after)` channels + safety-net `time.NewTimer(safetyIntervalEffective())`. Three constants: `safetyPollInterval = 12 * time.Second` (production cadence when a subscriber is wired), `legacyPollInterval = 2500 * time.Millisecond` (no-subscriber fallback for PTY-unavailable hosts and tests), `metricsPollInterval = 2500 * time.Millisecond` (decoupled from SSE intervals). New `WindowChangeSubscriber` interface in `api/sse.go`; production adapter `supervisorSubscriber` (`api/tmuxctl_bridge.go`) forwards `Generation`/`Wait` to `Supervisor.Get(server)` and returns a never-closing channel via `neverChan()` when no Client exists for a server (avoids busy-looping when control-mode is unavailable). `hubSink` shipped here as a no-op `tmuxctl.EventSink` (the hub observed change via the Client's generation counter) — *superseded by `260530-v6hm-active-window-event-derivation`, which made `hubSink` record per-group active-window state into a per-Client `ActiveWindowTracker`; the generation-counter path is unchanged*; `Server.SetWindowChangeSubscriber(sub)` lazy-initialises the hub and stores the subscriber. `detectKilledWindowIDs(prev, current)` extracted as a pure function in `api/sse.go` and called from the snapshot-build site (no longer inline in the poll loop); `previousWindowIDs[server]` initialised on the first snapshot per server so cold-start emits no synthetic `board-changed { cleanup }` events. `selectFirst` reads `timer.C` in the outer select to avoid a goroutine-leak where the timer would otherwise block on `Stop`-without-drain. Heartbeat moved to wall-clock cadence (`sseHeartbeatPeriod = 15s`) so the slower safety interval doesn't starve heartbeats. `cmd/rk/serve.go` orders startup as `tmux.EnsureConfig → sweepOrphanedRelaySessions → tmuxctl.NewSupervisor + Start → server.ListenAndServe`; sweep before subscribe so the `_rk-ctl` anchor isn't observed as an orphan. Graceful shutdown stops the Supervisor with a 5s bounded context BEFORE `server.Shutdown`; stop errors logged at `slog.Warn` and never block shutdown. New dep: `github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify`. Constitution alignment: §I (`exec.CommandContext` with argument slices; no shell strings), §II (no persistent state — Supervisor map mirrors kernel-observable sockets), §VI (passive observation, never mutates user sessions), no new network surface. (nvlp)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix(sync): event-driven active window via tmux control mode (#198)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — `_rk-ctl` hidden anchor session for tmuxctl control mode. New exported `tmux.ControlAnchorSessionName = "_rk-ctl"` constant (alongside `RelaySessionPrefix` in `internal/tmux/tmux.go`); `parseSessions` early-skips any line whose `#{session_name}` equals it — single chokepoint mirroring the existing `rk-relay-*` skip, so every consumer (REST `/api/sessions`, SSE `event: sessions`, board derivation, server-aggregate `/api/servers`) automatically excludes the anchor. `app/backend/internal/tmuxctl/Client` creates the anchor via `tmux [-L <socket>] new-session -d -s _rk-ctl` when the target tmux server has zero pre-existing sessions (`tmux -CC` requires an attached session to emit notifications), then tags it with the server-scoped user-option `@rk_ctl_keepalive 1` via `set-option -t =_rk-ctl @rk_ctl_keepalive 1` (exported as `tmuxctl.AnchorKeepaliveOption`; defensive marker only, no v1 runtime consumer). Concurrent-rk "duplicate session" error treated as benign by `isDuplicateSessionError`. The Client invokes `tmux -CC attach-session -t =<bootstrap> -r` with the anchored `=` target form (PR #196 convention) and the `-r` read-only flag (restricts input, notifications still emit). `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions` is excluded by construction (matches only the `rk-relay-` prefix), with an explicit `name == ControlAnchorSessionName` guard added for defense-in-depth. Anchor outlives any single Client lifetime; only `tmux kill-server` removes it. (nvlp)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix(sync): event-driven active window via tmux control mode (#198)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — URL as resumable bookmark; tmux is the truth for current window. New § URL as Resumable Bookmark documents the post-change model: tmux drives selection; the URL is consulted on mount only and rewritten by an SSE-driven `navigate({ replace: true })` whenever `currentSession.windows.find(w => w.isActiveWindow).index` differs from `windowIndex`. Mount-time alignment in `app.tsx` fires exactly one `selectWindow(server, session, Number(urlWindow))` per session-route mount, guarded by `hasAlignedToUrlRef: useRef(false)` plus `lastAlignedSessionRef` (resets the guard when `${server}|${sessionName}` changes). `navigateToWindow(session, windowIdx)` simplified to call `selectWindow` only — no `navigate` at click time; URL follows on the next SSE snapshot (typically sub-500ms via the new tmuxctl control-mode subscription). The 3-second `userNavTimestampRef` / `elapsed < 3000` debounce was deleted entirely (it was protecting client-owned selection state that no longer exists). `dialogOpenRef` retained so the URL writeback effect skips navigation mid-dialog (prevents focus-stealing re-renders). `WindowRow.isSelected` derives from `(!ghost && win.isActiveWindow) || currentWindowIndex === String(win.index)` — primary driver is the server-reported `isActiveWindow`; URL fallback covers ghost windows mid-creation and the initial render before the first SSE payload. Multi-client convergence: same-server tabs yank to the new window via the writeback effect; board-route tabs do NOT navigate; different-server tabs are independent. Stale-URL deep-link tabs yank existing tabs as a deliberate side effect of mount-time alignment. Supersedes the 2026-03-07 `history.replaceState` + SSE-polling sync model. (nvlp)

## 2026-05-09
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Rotated shell layout (CSS Grid topology)** — Shell wrapper at `app/frontend/src/components/shell/shell.tsx` shared by `AppShell` and `BoardPage`. Desktop grid `"sidebar topbar" / "sidebar content" / "sidebar bottombar"` (sidebar full-height, spans 3 rows); columns `${sidebarWidth}px 1fr` ↔ `0 1fr` with `grid-template-columns 150ms ease-out`. Mobile (`< 640px`) collapses to single-column grid; sidebar renders as a grid-row-spanning overlay (`gridRow: "2/4"`, `position: absolute` not `fixed`) with backdrop, `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"` — preserves topbar visibility per existing project convention. Hamburger statically rendered at `TopBar.left` in both states (no component-level relocation; visual movement is a side effect of the column collapse). `Cmd+\` / `Ctrl+\` toggles `sidebarOpen` (registered in `KeyboardShortcuts` modal); chord suppressed on real text inputs but NOT on xterm helper textareas. `ChromeContext` shape changed: `sidebarOpen`/`sidebarWidth` lifted from per-route state; `drawerOpen`/`setDrawerOpen` removed (single boolean covers both desktop column and mobile overlay). New dispatch surface `setSidebarWidth` (in-memory, per-pointermove) + `persistSidebarWidth` (one localStorage write at drag-end) splits live updates from persistence. New `FocusedTerminalContext` (`app/frontend/src/contexts/focused-terminal-context.tsx`) tracks the focused terminal across routes — `TerminalClient` registers on mount via `registerFocus` prop, `BoardPane` registers on focus events (click, cycle, initial pane). `BottomBar` moves up to shell-level (rendered once per route by both AppShell and BoardPage); `wsRef` prop removed in favor of `focused?.wsRef` from context. BoardPage gets a BottomBar for the first time, byte-identical to AppShell's invocation. `composeOpen` lifted to `FocusedTerminalContext` so the shell-level BottomBar can open compose for the focused pane on any route; the focused pane's `TerminalClient` gates `<ComposeBuffer>` rendering on `isFocused && composeOpen`. Compose target frozen at open-time via existing `wsRef` snapshot. `TopBar` accepts `mode: "terminal" | "board" | "root"` (default `"terminal"`); board mode renders `Board ▸ {name} ▾` breadcrumb (the existing `BoardSwitcherDropdown`, now imported by TopBar) + inline info `{N} pane[s] · {M} server[s] · ⌘[⌘] cycle` (`hidden sm:inline`). `FixedWidthToggle` lifted out of the `currentWindow &&` block — now route-agnostic. BoardPage's bespoke `h-screen w-screen flex` root and inline mini-header are gone. Provider tree order is now `ThemeProvider > ChromeProvider > RootWrapper(SessionProvider > OptimisticProvider > FocusedTerminalProvider > Outlet)`. Frontend-only — backend untouched. (17m3)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Multi-server SessionProvider + unified Sidebar** — `SessionProvider` (`app/frontend/src/contexts/session-context.tsx`) promoted from a single-server, layout-mounted provider into a root-mounted multi-server provider. State shape switched to per-server keyed `Map`s: `sessionsByServer`, `sessionOrderByServer`, `isConnectedByServer`, `metricsByServer`, plus `currentServer: string | null` dispatched from the matched route via `useMatches()` (`params.server` for `/$server/...`, `null` for `/board/$name` and `/`). One `EventSource` per server in the pool — current server eager, others via `attachServer(name)`; per-server 3-second disconnect debounce; pool entries closed and map keys removed when `/api/servers` drops a server. `subscribeBoardChange` API multiplexes cross-server `board-changed` events through the same pool so `useBoards` and `useWindowPins` consume them without opening their own connections. Provider mounts in `RootWrapper` above `<Outlet />`; `ServerShell` becomes a thin layout (its `useMatches()` server extraction is consumed only inside AppShell). Sidebar (`app/frontend/src/components/sidebar/index.tsx`) renders one `ServerGroup` per server in `servers` (each a `CollapsiblePanel`-style collapsible) with the current server's group default-open and others default-collapsed; toggles persist per-server in `localStorage["runkit-panel-sessions-{server}"]` (best-effort migration of the legacy `runkit-panel-sessions` key into the current server's slot on first read when `currentServer` is non-null). Current-server header carries the Server panel's selected-tile shade (driven by `serverColors` + `rowTints`); no marker on board routes. Cross-server window navigation: clicking a session/window in a non-current server's tree calls `navigate({ to: "/$server/$session/$window", params: { server: thatServer, ... } })`. Cross-server window drag-and-drop is rejected with toast `"Moving windows across tmux servers isn't supported yet"` (within-server reorder/move unchanged). BoardPage (`app/frontend/src/components/board/board-page.tsx`) drops its mini-sidebar (former `<aside>` lines 202-234) and renders the unified `<Sidebar currentServer={null} ...>`; the `← Sessions` back-link is removed (sessions are reachable via the unified sidebar's per-server groups or the board top-bar `▾` switcher). Provider tree order is now `ThemeProvider > ChromeProvider > RootWrapper(SessionProvider > OptimisticProvider > Outlet)`. All 9 `useSessionContext` consumers consume the keyed shape directly — no transitional accessor remains. Tests: `session-context.test.tsx` rewritten with multi-instance `MockEventSource` (keyed by URL) for per-server SSE isolation and lazy attach assertions; `sidebar.test.tsx` updated to the new prop surface and per-server group expectations; new `multi-server-sidebar.spec.ts` Playwright e2e + companion `.spec.md` spawns a second `rk-e2e-multi-{digits}` tmux server and asserts both render in the sidebar with cross-server navigation. Backend untouched (already per-server). (dc0t)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Relay grouped sessions for board panes** — `app/backend/api/relay.go` now creates a per-WebSocket ephemeral grouped session (`rk-relay-<8 hex>` from `crypto/rand`, via the new `tmux.NewGroupedSession` wrapper around `tmux new-session -d -s <ephemeral> -t <real>`) and attaches to the ephemeral rather than the real session. `defer s.tmux.KillSessionCtx(context.Background(), server, ephemeral)` reaps the ephemeral on every exit path (fresh `context.Background()` because `r.Context()` is cancelled at cleanup time). Fixes the central board bug — N panes targeting different windows of one tmux session now show distinct content because grouped sessions share window membership but maintain independent active-window state — and as a side effect fixes a latent two-tabs-same-session focus-stealing bug. New `tmux.NewGroupedSession`, `tmux.KillSessionCtx`, `tmux.RelaySessionPrefix = "rk-relay-"`, and `tmux.ListRawSessionNames` (unfiltered list-sessions for housekeeping callers); `parseSessions` filters `rk-relay-*` so ephemerals never leak into user-facing session lists at the single chokepoint. `TmuxOps` interface gains `NewGroupedSession` and `KillSessionCtx`; `mockTmuxOps` updated. Startup sweep `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions(ctx)` in `app/backend/cmd/rk/serve_sweep.go` reaps orphan ephemerals across every server returned by `tmux.ListServers(ctx)` before HTTP bind (synchronous, 30s bounded, per-server failures logged and skipped). New tests: `TestNewGroupedSession_*`, `parseSessions` filter cases, `app/backend/api/relay_test.go` (two-relay-distinct-output, missing-session-4004, ephemeral-cleanup-on-close), `boards-same-session-multi-pane.spec.ts` Playwright e2e + companion `.spec.md`. WebSocket URL contract `/relay/{session}/{window}?server={server}` unchanged — ephemeral name is purely backend-internal. (hdjr)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — Per-WebSocket ephemeral grouped sessions for the relay. New `tmux.NewGroupedSession(ctx, server, realSession, ephemeral)` wraps `tmux new-session -d -s <ephemeral> -t <realSession>` (with a `has-session` probe to avoid leaking empty-group sessions when the target is missing), `tmux.KillSessionCtx(ctx, server, session)` exposes a ctx-accepting kill so relay cleanup can pass `context.Background()` (existing `KillSession` becomes a thin wrapper), and `tmux.RelaySessionPrefix = "rk-relay-"` reserves the name space. `parseSessions` early-skips any `rk-relay-*` line — single chokepoint for all user-facing session lists (REST, SSE, board derivation, server-aggregate). New `tmux.ListRawSessionNames(ctx, server)` returns the unfiltered list for housekeeping callers (the startup sweep, which would otherwise be unable to see the very ephemerals it needs to reap). Relay handler (`api/relay.go`) generates the ephemeral name via `crypto/rand` (constitution I), creates the grouped session, defers `KillSessionCtx(context.Background(), …)` (request context is cancelled at cleanup time), then `select-window` and `attach-session` against the ephemeral — never against the real session. Fixes the central bug where N board panes targeting different windows of one tmux session showed identical content (tmux's per-session active-window state); fixes a latent two-tabs-same-session focus-stealing bug as a side effect. Startup sweep `sweepOrphanedRelaySessions(ctx)` in `cmd/rk/serve_sweep.go` reaps orphans across all `tmux.ListServers(ctx)` before HTTP bind (synchronous, 30s bounded; per-server failures logged and skipped). `TmuxOps` interface gains `NewGroupedSession` and `KillSessionCtx`; `mockTmuxOps` updated. WebSocket URL contract unchanged. (hdjr)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — **Rotated shell layout** — Shared `<Shell>` CSS Grid wrapper (`app/frontend/src/components/shell/shell.tsx`) used by AppShell and BoardPage; desktop topology `"sidebar topbar" / "sidebar content" / "sidebar bottombar"`, sidebar full-height. Sidebar collapses to 0px (no rail) with `grid-template-columns 150ms ease-out`; drag-resize handle hidden when collapsed. Hamburger statically at TopBar.left; visual relocation on collapse is a side effect of column collapse. `Cmd+\` / `Ctrl+\` toggles sidebar from any Shell-bearing route (registered in KeyboardShortcuts modal); chord suppressed on real text inputs but NOT on xterm helper textareas. Mobile (< 640px) collapses to single-column grid; sidebar renders as `position: absolute` overlay positioned via `gridRow: "2/4"` (below topbar, matches project convention) with `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"`. Sidebar section order is **Boards → Servers → Sessions** (was Servers → Boards → Sessions); BoardsSection is always visible with `Pin a window to start a board` hint when empty (was hidden when empty). New `FocusedTerminalContext` (`app/frontend/src/contexts/focused-terminal-context.tsx`) tracks focused terminal across routes; mounts in `RootWrapper`; producers are `TerminalClient` (registers via `registerFocus` prop) and `BoardPane` (registers on focus events, doesn't clear on focus loss). Compose state (`composeOpen`/`setComposeOpen`) lifted to the same context so the shell-level BottomBar can open compose for the focused pane on the board route; focused pane gates `<ComposeBuffer>` rendering on `isFocused && composeOpen`. BottomBar moves up to `<Shell>`'s `bottombar` grid area — rendered once per route by AppShell and BoardPage (board route gets a BottomBar for the first time). `BottomBar.wsRef` prop removed; reads `focused?.wsRef` from context. `TopBar` accepts `mode: "terminal" | "board" | "root"` prop; board mode renders breadcrumb + inline-info `{N} pane[s] · {M} server[s] · ⌘[⌘] cycle` (hidden on `< 640px`). FixedWidthToggle lifted out of the `currentWindow &&` block — now route-agnostic. `ChromeContext` shape: `sidebarOpen`/`sidebarWidth` lifted from per-route state; `drawerOpen`/`setDrawerOpen` removed. New `setSidebarWidth` (in-memory) + `persistSidebarWidth` (localStorage at drag-end) split. BoardPage's bespoke `h-screen w-screen flex` root and inline mini-header are gone; BoardSwitcherDropdown moves into TopBar board-mode rendering. Three pattern captures: shared chrome wrapper for related routes (`<Shell>`), lift focused-input target to context, two-setter pair for in-memory + persisted state. (17m3)

## 2026-05-07
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat(sidebar): persist session reorder to tmux user-option (#185)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Pane boards** — Cross-server pane dashboard at `/board/$name` (root-level peer to `/$server`, not under server). New `internal/tmux/board.go` (parse/serialize `@rk_board`, fractional indexing, lazy stale cleanup, idempotent `Pin`/`Unpin`/`Reorder`, `RemoveAllByWindowID`). New `api/boards.go` with five endpoints (`GET /api/boards`, `GET /api/boards/{name}`, `POST /api/boards/{name}/{pin,unpin,reorder}`); routes registered in `router.go`; `TmuxOps` extended; `mockTmuxOps` updated. `sseHub` extended with `BoardEntriesFetcher` interface, `previousBoardJSON` cache, `broadcastBoardChanged`, `event: board-changed` (kebab-case), once-per-server bootstrap on first poll, cached-snapshot replay to new clients, and window-kill eager cleanup that fires one `cleanup` event per affected board. Frontend: `app/frontend/src/api/boards.ts` (`listBoards`/`getBoard` via `deduplicatedFetch`, `pinWindow`/`unpinWindow`/`reorderPin` mutations with `server` first-arg per the routing contract); `useBoards()` + `useBoardEntries(name)` hooks in `app/frontend/src/hooks/use-boards.ts` (50ms-debounced cross-server SSE re-fetch, error tolerance preserves last good value); `usePinActions`, `usePaneWidths`, `useIsMobile`, `useActiveBoardName`, `useWindowPins` companion hooks; `BoardPage`/`BoardPane`/`BoardHeader` mini-layout (own sidebar + topbar + `<CommandPalette>` mount); `/board/$name` route in `router.tsx` peer to `serverLayoutRoute`. Sidebar `BoardsSection` (above Sessions, hidden when zero boards), pin icon + popover on `WindowRow`, active-board accent border on pinned rows when current route is `/board/<name>`. `boardActions` `PaletteAction[]` in `app.tsx` between `windowActions` and `viewActions` (Switch/Pin/Unpin Current Window only on AppShell; Switch + Leave + Cycle Pane Focus on the BoardPage palette mount; Reorder Pane deferred to v1.1). Pane widths persisted per-board to `localStorage["runkit:board-widths:<name>"]`; pin state persists in tmux. Mobile: single-pane swipe carousel below `min-width: 640px`. (4vuv)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Server-persisted sidebar session order** — Sidebar session reorder now lives in tmux server-scoped user-option `@rk_session_order` (JSON-encoded array of session names), not localStorage, so the order syncs across all browser tabs/devices connected to the same tmux server. New `tmux.GetSessionOrder(ctx, server)` / `tmux.SetSessionOrder(ctx, server, order)` wrappers in `internal/tmux/tmux.go` (both apply `context.WithTimeout(ctx, TmuxTimeout)` and route through `tmuxExecRawServer`, which now also captures stderr in error returns so callers can pattern-match on tmux's "invalid option" / "no server running" diagnostics — `CapturePane` was the only other caller; transparent change). New endpoints `GET /api/sessions/order` (returns `{order:[...]}` — empty on unset/no-server) and `PUT /api/sessions/order` (validates each name via `validate.ValidateName`, persists, then synchronously broadcasts a new `event: session-order` SSE frame). SSE hub (`api/sse.go`) gains `previousOrderJSON` cache, `broadcastSessionOrder(server, order)` method, an `addClient` cached-snapshot send (positioned between `sessions` and `metrics`), and a once-per-server bootstrap in `poll()` that reads tmux via an injected `SessionOrderFetcher` interface (production wires `prodSessionOrderFetcher{}`; tests substitute a stub). `TmuxOps` interface gains the two methods; `mockTmuxOps` updated accordingly. Frontend: new `getSessionOrder` / `setSessionOrder` API client methods (first-arg `server` per the routing contract), `SessionContext` extended with a `sessionOrder: string[]` slice populated from the SSE event, sidebar (`sidebar/index.tsx`) implements drag-and-drop reorder with custom MIME `application/x-session-reorder` (so it doesn't collide with the existing window-drag-to-session feature), 250ms trailing-debounced PUT (one HTTP call per drag op), and a `localOrder` override that survives until the next SSE-delivered `sessionOrder` change (so the UI doesn't snap back to the pre-PUT order during the round-trip). `SessionRow` gains optional `draggable` / `isDragSource` / `onDragStart` / `onDragEnd` props (`opacity-50` while dragging; ghost/optimistic sessions are NOT draggable). `StandaloneSessionContextProvider` added for tests so existing `Sidebar` tests don't need to wrap with the EventSource-opening `SessionProvider`. New tests: `TestGetSessionOrder_*` / `TestSetSessionOrder_*` (5 tmux integration tests, gated on `command -v tmux`); `TestSessionOrder_GET_*` / `TestSessionOrder_PUT_*` (8 handler tests including `_triggersBroadcast` end-to-end via direct hub injection); `TestSSE_BroadcastSessionOrderReachesMatchingClients` / `_SessionOrderCachedOnConnect` / `_HubBootstrapReadsOrderOnFirstPoll`; `client.test.ts` extended; `session-context.test.tsx` (4 SSE-handler tests); Playwright e2e `session-reorder.spec.ts` (PUT + reload + assert order survives). Constitution alignment: I (security — names validated, args slice never shell-interpolated), II (no DB — option lives in tmux), III (process timeouts — explicit `context.WithTimeout(ctx, TmuxTimeout)` on every new tmux call), VI (tmux survives rk-go restart — bootstrap closes the cache-cold-start gap), VII (convention over configuration — uses existing `@rk_*` user-option pattern, existing `withServer` first-arg client convention). (lvon)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — feat(sidebar): persist session reorder to tmux user-option (#185)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — `@rk_board` server-scoped option for pane boards. New `internal/tmux/board.go` exposes `ListBoardEntries`, `ListAllBoardEntries`, `ListBoards`, `GetBoard`, `Pin`, `Unpin`, `Reorder`, `RemoveAllByWindowID`, and `ComputeOrderKey`. Distributed storage: each tmux server stores entries only for its own windows; aggregate views read from every server returned by `ListServers()` and union/tag entries with their source server. Value format: comma-separated `<window_id>:<board>:<order_key>` entries (`,` and `:` reserved separators excluded by the board-name regex `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,32}$`). Order keys use lowercase-`a..z` fractional indexing — inserts never renumber. Lazy stale-entry cleanup at read time intersects with `liveWindowIDs(server)` and best-effort writes back the cleaned slice (read still returns success on write-back failure). Eager cleanup via the SSE poll-tick: window-kill detection scans per-server entries and calls `RemoveAllByWindowID`, then broadcasts one `event: board-changed { change: "cleanup" }` per affected board. SSE hub bootstraps `@rk_board` once per server on first poll and emits a synthetic `board-changed { change: "bootstrap", entries: [...] }` so an rk-go restart with tmux still running rehydrates the boards UI (parity with `@rk_session_order`). All wrappers route through `tmuxExecRawServer` with `context.WithTimeout(ctx, TmuxTimeout)`. (4vuv)

Pane cards: 480px default, drag-resize 280px–viewport-minus-sidebar, widths persisted per-board to `localStorage["runkit:board-widths:<name>"]`. Click-to-focus + `Cmd+]/Cmd+[` cycle (hover-to-focus OFF in v1). Mobile (< 640px): single-pane swipe carousel with off-screen pause via `TerminalClient` unmount + pagination dots. Sidebar gains `BoardsSection` above Sessions (hidden when zero boards, hint mode when on a now-empty board route), pin icon + `PinPopover` on `WindowRow` (filled state when pinned to ANY board), and active-board accent border on rows pinned to the current board. New `boardActions` `PaletteAction[]` block in `app.tsx`: `Switch to <name>` (one per board, `(current)` on active), `Pin Current Window` (window-route-gated, dispatches `pin-popover:open`), `Unpin Current Window` (unpins from all boards), `Leave Board View`, `Cycle Pane Focus →/←` (board-route-gated). Reorder Pane palette action deferred to v1.1; right-click context menu not implemented (use sidebar pin icon, command palette, or board pane header). Hooks: `useBoards`, `useBoardEntries`, `usePinActions`, `usePaneWidths`, `useIsMobile`, `useActiveBoardName`, `useWindowPins`. (4vuv)

## 2026-05-06
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — fix(rk): rename --fan-out to --count/-N and fix fan-out bugs (#184)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — `--count` rename and fan-out correctness fixes. (1) Hard-rename `--fan-out N` → `--count N` (short form `-N`, uppercase) — no alias, no deprecation warning; `--fan-out` is now an unknown flag. Internal renames track the user-facing flag: `riffFanOutFlag` → `riffCountFlag`, `effectiveSpec.FanOut` → `effectiveSpec.Count`, `runFanOut` → `runCount`. The parallelism-mechanic helpers (`fanOutResult`, `planFanOutRollback`, `rollbackFanOut`, `rollbackPlan`) keep their `fanOut`/`Plan` naming because they describe the mechanic, not the flag. (2) Bug fix: pane focus targets the captured pane id from `tmux new-window -P -F '#{pane_id}'` rather than a hardcoded `<window>.0` index, which was wrong on tmux configs with `pane-base-index 1` (every riff invocation printed `can't find pane: 0` on stderr). New helpers: `buildNewWindowCaptureArgs` (pure argv shape), `parsePaneID` (pure stdout parser — single trimmed line), `runTmuxNewWindowCapturePaneID` (subprocess with `tmuxTimeout`, parent context propagation, `tmuxChildEnv()`). `buildSpawnArgvs` no longer emits the trailing `select-pane` row — pane id is a runtime value, so that step is constructed by the orchestrator (`spawnRiffReturningName`). (3) Bug fix: `runWtDelete` now invokes `wt delete --non-interactive <name>` (positional name; deprecated `--worktree-name` removed; `--non-interactive` suppresses the `Delete this worktree?` prompt that previously caused silent rollback failures because rollback runs without a tty). Argv shape extracted to pure helper `buildWtDeleteArgs`. (4) New tests: `TestParsePaneID`, `TestBuildNewWindowCaptureArgs`, `TestRiffCountShortForm` (`-N`/`--count`/`--count=`/default), `TestRiffFanOutFlagRejected` (regression-protect the rename), `TestBuildWtDeleteArgs` (regression-protect the `--non-interactive` + positional-name argv). `TestBuildSpawnArgvs` updated to drop the `select-pane` row expectation. (lald)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix(rk): rename --fan-out to --count/-N and fix fan-out bugs (#184)

## 2026-04-23
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: rk riff — Workflow Features (pane arrays, layouts, presets, fan-out) (#177)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — feat: rk riff — Workflow Features (pane arrays, layouts, presets, fan-out) (#177)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — refactor: rk riff — CLI Surface Refinement (#175)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — fix: rk riff — correctness and portability fixes (#174)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — Correctness and portability fixes — no CLI surface change. (1) New pure `shellWrap(cmd)` helper appends `; exec "${SHELL:-/bin/sh}"`; used by both new-window and split paths so panes stay interactive after their commands exit. (2) Launcher now runs under an interactive `${SHELL:-/bin/sh} -i -c '...'` wrap inside `buildNewWindowArgs` so `.zshrc`/`.bashrc` aliases, functions, and interactive-only PATH tweaks reach the Claude Code launcher (closes Bug 3). (3) Split pane replaces the hardcoded `exec zsh` with `shellWrap(setupCmd)` — bash/fish users land in their own shell (closes Bug 8). (4) SIGINT/SIGTERM propagation — `runRiff` wraps its root context via `signal.NotifyContext(cmd.Context(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)` with `defer stop()`, threaded through all three subprocess call sites; Ctrl-C no longer leaves orphan `wt`/`tmux` children (closes Bug 10). (5) Window-name auto-suffix — `listWindowNames(ctx)` probes `tmux list-windows -F '#W'` and `resolveWindowName(existing, base)` (pure, gap-filling) applies `-2`, `-3`, … on collision; TOCTOU race between list and new-window is accepted (closes Bug 11). (6) Added Security / Trust Boundary section documenting `fab/project/config.yaml` as committed-code-equivalent and naming launcher shell expansion as the intentional exception to constitution §I (addresses Bug 9 via docs only — no code mitigation). `buildNewWindowArgs` signature changed to accept the resolved name; new `TestShellWrap` and `TestResolveWindowName` cover the new pure helpers. (ba9f)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — Workflow features — pane-array model, layouts, presets, and fan-out. (1) `--skill` and `--cmd` are now repeatable with argv-ordered pane composition; a custom `paneFlag` pflag.Value + argv pre-processor (`rewritePaneSpaceForm`) support bare / space-form / equals-form syntax uniformly. Pane 0 always gets tmux focus via `select-pane -t <window>.0`. (2) `--layout` flag accepts 6 canonical tmux names + 6 shortforms (`a`/`t`/`h`/`v`/`deck-h`/`deck-v`), resolved via `layoutAliases`; `auto` dispatches by pane count via `autoLayout`. Unicode box-drawing mockups render inline in `rk riff -h` via `layout_help.go`. (3) `--setup-pane` removed outright (no alias, no deprecation warning) — `--cmd` subsumes it. (4) Presets under `riff.presets.<name>` in `fab/project/config.yaml` invokable positionally (`rk riff ship`) or via `--preset`; panes are a typed ordered list of `{skill|cmd: value}` entries; CLI panes replace preset panes entirely; CLI layout overrides preset layout; preset `wt_args` are prepended to user passthrough. Preset with a pane having both `skill` and `cmd` keys is silently discarded (best-effort posture matching `ReadSpawnCommand`). (5) `--list-presets` short-circuits before preconditions and prints presets in YAML source order. (6) `--fan-out N` spawns N parallel worktree/window pairs sharing one pane shape; failure triggers rollback via `wt delete` + `tmux kill-window` for the non-failing goroutines' artifacts (computed by pure `planFanOutRollback`). `fabconfig.ReadPresets` + `ReadPresetsOrdered` added. New pure helpers: `resolveActivePreset`, `resolveEffectiveSpec`, `buildSpawnArgvs`, `buildSkillShellString`, `buildCmdShellString`, `planFanOutRollback`. (jmwu)
- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — CLI surface refinement — hard-rename flags and expand help text, no behavioral change. (1) `--cmd` renamed to `--skill` (hard-rename, no deprecation alias — invocations using `--cmd` fail with cobra's "unknown flag" error). (2) `--split` renamed to `--setup-pane` (hard-rename, no deprecation alias). (3) Package-level Go variables renamed in lockstep: `riffCmdFlag` → `riffSkillFlag`, `riffSplitFlag` → `riffSetupPaneFlag`. (4) `Use:` synopsis expanded from `riff [-- wt-flags...]` to `riff [--skill <name>] [--setup-pane <cmd>] [-- <wt-flags>...]`. (5) `Long:` help expanded to match `serve.go` house style. (6) Bug 2 investigation note — verified positional argv to `claude` dispatches slash-commands correctly. (udhe)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — feat: rk riff — Workflow Features (pane arrays, layouts, presets, fan-out) (#177)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix: rk riff — correctness and portability fixes (#174)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — `rk riff` window creation now multi-pane + fan-out. Each window is built by a `tmux new-window` + N-1 `tmux split-window` sequence, followed by `tmux select-layout <canonical>` and `tmux select-pane -t <window>.0` (focus pane 0 per the argv-order rule). Skill panes preserve the three-layer `<launcher> '<escaped-skill>'` → `sh -i -c` → `shellWrap` composition from earlier changes; cmd panes skip the interactive wrap (would alter user-command argv). Fan-out with N ≥ 2 spawns N goroutines sharing one context; failure triggers rollback via the pure `planFanOutRollback(results, failureIdx)` builder + `wt delete` / `tmux kill-window` calls against a fresh (non-cancelled) context so rollback runs to completion. Worktree names come from `wt`'s generator (no rk-side `-1..-N` scheme); windows named `riff-<wt-basename>` with `resolveWindowName` suffix fallback on collision. `--setup-pane`/`--split` removed entirely — `--cmd` (repeatable) subsumes the single-split use case. (jmwu)

## 2026-04-20
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — fix: Visual Display Recipe documents relative proxy URL for rk iframes (#170)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Visual Display Recipe canonical in `rk context`** — New `### Visual Display Recipe` subsection in `cmd/rk/context.go` `writeCapabilities` output, placed between `### Proxy` and `### CLI Commands`. Documents the 4-step iframe-display flow: generate HTML → loopback `python3 -m http.server --bind 127.0.0.1` → iframe window with **relative** `@rk_url /proxy/<port>/<filename>` → fail-silent on missing prerequisites. Relative path is deliberate — the run-kit frontend resolves it against the user's browser origin, so the recipe works identically direct or behind a reverse proxy (matches the existing pattern in `app/frontend/src/components/iframe-window.tsx:119`). Two new Go tests (`TestVisualDisplayRecipeOrdering`, `TestVisualDisplayRecipeNoAbsoluteProxyURL`) plus 3 assertions in `TestContextCapabilitiesSections`. Cross-repo coordination: fab-kit `src/kit/skills/_preamble.md` `### Visual Display Recipe` collapsed from inline 4-step prose (which had drifted to an absolute `{server_url}/proxy/...` form) to a pointer at `rk context` output — eliminates the drift class. fab-kit commit local only; propagates to `.claude/skills/_preamble/SKILL.md` deployed copies on next `fab sync` after fab-kit release. (l1ja)

## 2026-04-19
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: prefer agent-bearing pane entries in fab pane-map dedup (#169)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Adapt to fab-kit v1.5.0 `_agents` schema** — pane-map dedup rule extended with rule-2 (`Change > AgentState > first-seen`) so discussion-mode agent panes (populated `AgentState`, nil `Change`) win over bare sibling panes in split windows. Dedup logic extracted from `fetchPaneMap` into standalone `dedupEntries(entries) map[string]paneMapEntry` helper in `app/backend/internal/sessions/sessions.go`. Agent state (`agentState`/`agentIdleDuration`) now resolves independently of change state (`fabChange`/`fabStage`) — either can populate or be empty on any given window. Two new tests: `TestPaneMapDedupPrefersAgentState` (both iteration orders) and `TestPaneMapDedupChangeStillWinsOverAgent` (rule-1 preservation). No frontend changes required — existing branching in `status-panel.tsx` and `format.ts` already treats the two fields independently. (afyv)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: Sidebar Separator Cursor Fixes (#168)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Sidebar separator cursor polish + corner resize affordance — horizontal separator cursor changed from `cursor-ns-resize` to `cursor-row-resize` (matches vertical's `cursor-col-resize` double-arrow-with-bar vocabulary); vertical hover fixed from `/40` opacity to full `hover:bg-text-secondary`. Both drag handlers now write `document.body.style.cursor` on pointerdown/start and clear to `""` on pointerup/end — document-level override survives the pointer leaving the thin handle (solves implicit-pointer-capture hover loss after first drag). `CollapsiblePanel` unmount cleanup also clears body cursor. New optional corner affordance at separator intersection: `CollapsiblePanel` gains `onCornerPointerDown` prop; when supplied + `showDragHandle` is true, renders a `w-[7px] h-[3px] cursor-nwse-resize` corner flush against the handle's right edge. Corner pointerdown invokes horizontal then vertical handlers, then overrides cursor to `nwse-resize` (last-write-wins). Handlers coexist without coordination because they use independent document listeners (clientY-only vs clientX-only). Prop threaded `app.tsx` → `Sidebar` (`onSidebarResizeStart`) → `ServerPanel` → `CollapsiblePanel` (`onCornerPointerDown`); all optional, mobile drawer omits it. (9ufu)

## 2026-04-18
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: Server panel tile grid (#159)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Server panel tile grid** — `/api/servers` response changed from `[]string` to `[]ServerInfo` (`{name, sessionCount}`). Session counts computed server-side via concurrent `tmux.ListSessions(ctx, server)` fan-out (`sync.WaitGroup` + mutex-protected results map); per-server errors logged and yield `sessionCount: 0` (no 5xx). Response sorted alphabetically. `TmuxOps` interface gains `ListSessions(ctx, server)`. Frontend `listServers()` returns `Promise<ServerInfo[]>`; `ServerInfo` exported from `app/frontend/src/api/client.ts`. All consumers (`SessionProvider`, `Sidebar`, `ServerPanel`, `ServerListPage`, `app.tsx` palette) read `.name`. `ServerListPage` cards show `{sessionCount} sess`. (jpkl)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — fix: thread tmux server through mutation API to fix closure race (#166)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — Frontend server-routing contract — `server: string` now threaded as the first positional argument through every `api/client.ts` function that hits a server-scoped endpoint (`getSessions`, `createSession`, `renameSession`, `killSession`, `createWindow`, `renameWindow`, `killWindow`, `moveWindow`, `moveWindowToSession`, `selectWindow`, `splitWindow`, `closePane`, `sendKeys`, `updateWindowUrl`, `updateWindowType`, `setWindowColor`, `setSessionColor`, `reloadTmuxConfig`, `uploadFile`, `getKeybindings`). `withServer(url, server)` is pure — the module-level `_getServer` global and `setServerGetter` export are removed; `SessionProvider` no longer wires a getter. Server-management endpoints (`listServers`, `createServer`, `killServer`) and theme/server-color settings remain server-parameter-free by design. Eliminates a closure-race where mid-action server switches retargeted in-flight rename/kill/create calls at the wrong tmux server. Mirrors the backend `tmuxExecServer(ctx, server, …)` shape. (yadg)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: thread tmux server through mutation API to fix closure race (#166)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: align xterm cell widths with tmux via Unicode 15 graphemes addon (#164)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: Sidebar header affordances — collapsed tint + Sessions name (#165)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — refactor: right-align server name in Server panel header (#163)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: Server panel tile grid (#159)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Right-align server name in ServerPanel header — `ServerPanel` title changed from dynamic `Tmux · {server}` to static `"Server"` (matches WindowPanel/HostPanel convention). Active server name moved into `headerRight` slot with `truncate text-text-primary font-mono` classes (mirrors `host-panel.tsx`); `LogoSpinner` follows the name when `refreshing`. Left-side chevron and title are now visually fixed across server switches — only the right-slot text updates. Playwright spec `server-panel-grid.spec.ts` and its companion `.spec.md` updated to match the new `name: /^Server/` accessible name and `Resize Server panel` separator label. No new patterns introduced — aligns with existing sidebar panel header convention. (2cjc)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Server panel tile grid + resizable CollapsiblePanel — `ServerPanel` rewritten from vertical list to swatch-style tile grid (`repeat(auto-fill, minmax(72px, 1fr))` desktop, single-row horizontal scroll with `scroll-snap-type` on `pointer: coarse` / `<640px`). Tiles: 4px ANSI-tinted top stripe + 11px truncated name + 10px "N sess" meta. Active tile: `aria-current` + inset accent ring + `rowTints.get(color).selected` body tint. Hover-revealed color-picker and kill buttons rendered as siblings to the tile `<button>` (avoids nested-button HTML) with `group-hover:flex`; hidden on coarse pointer. Scrolls internally when tile grid overflows the user-set height. `CollapsiblePanel` gained opt-in `resizable`, `defaultHeight`, `minHeight`, `maxHeight`, `mobileHeight` props: 6px `ns-resize` drag handle persisted to `localStorage[${storageKey}-height]`, height clamping, `calc(100vh - Npx)` maxHeight parsing, mobile drag-handle hide. Window/Host panels unchanged (opt-in preserves legacy behaviour). `/api/servers` now returns `{name, sessionCount}[]` per architecture.md. (jpkl)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — xterm Unicode 15 grapheme widths — added `@xterm/addon-unicode-graphemes` to the Terminal init chain (loads after WebLinks, before WebGL), set `allowProposedApi: true` on the Terminal constructor, and assigned `terminal.unicode.activeVersion = "15-graphemes"` after `loadAddon()`. Aligns xterm's cell-width measurements with tmux's wcwidth-based layout so emojis and other wide graphemes (ZWJ sequences, flag/skin-tone modifiers) render without ghost/overlap artifacts. The `unicodeVersion` constructor option remains a no-op past `"6"` without the addon. (xgl2)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Server-capture-at-trigger convention for optimistic actions — every `useOptimisticAction` instance for a server-scoped mutation now threads `server: string` as the first slot of its argument tuple (Shape B), with `server` read from `useSessionContext()` and listed in the calling handler's `useCallback` deps (Shape A). Async-bridge refs (`lastKillSessionRef`, `lastRenameSessionRef`, `killDialogServerRef`) snapshot `{ server, name }` together so rollback/settle target the originating server. `OptimisticContext` switched session-level `GhostEntry`/`KilledEntry`/`RenamedEntry` to discriminated unions carrying `server`; `markKilled`/`unmarkKilled` overloaded by `type` ("session" requires `server`, "server" is global); `useMergedSessions(real, currentServer)` now filters session-level overlays so cross-server overlays don't leak. Window-store keying unchanged — windows don't migrate across servers. Establishes the rule: ambient module-level state for request parameters is prohibited; request-scoping values travel in the call signature, captured at user-event time. Regression test in `use-dialog-state.test.tsx` flips `SessionProvider.server` between dialog open and submit. (yadg)

(5nok)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Kill-redirect SSE-replay gate** — `computeKillRedirect` (`app/frontend/src/lib/navigation.ts`) gains `currentWindowEverSeen?: boolean` param (defaults to `true`). When false, all "gone" branches short-circuit to `null`, deferring the session-dashboard / nearest-sibling redirect until the URL target has been observed as valid at least once. `app.tsx` tracks the seen state via `currentWindowEverSeenRef` (flipped true whenever memoized `currentWindow` is non-null) plus `lastObservedUrlKeyRef` (string `${server}|${session}|${window}`, resets the seen flag on URL change). Eliminates a startup race where a stale-cached first SSE payload or a session with briefly-empty `windows` array (while tmux enumeration propagates) redirected to the dashboard and unmounted `TerminalClient` mid-init — surfaced as `mobile-touch-scroll.spec.ts` flakes where `.xterm-screen` briefly appeared then disappeared. Two new unit tests in `navigation.test.ts` for the stale-SSE and empty-windows-transient cases. No changes to `TerminalClient` or SSE hub. (g9uo)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **`rk riff` — worktree + tmux window + Claude launcher** — New Cobra subcommand `riff` in `cmd/rk/riff.go` unifies the `riff`/`riffs` shell functions into a first-class `rk` command. Wraps `wt create --non-interactive --worktree-open skip [passthrough...]`, parses the `Path:` line, then runs `tmux new-window -c <path> "<launcher> '<cmd>'"` (optionally followed by `tmux split-window -h -c <path> "<setup>; exec zsh"` when `--split` is non-empty). Launcher resolved via new `internal/fabconfig/` package (`ReadSpawnCommand(repoRoot)` — best-effort `yaml.v3` read of `agent.spawn_command` from `fab/project/config.yaml`, falls back to `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`). Repo-root discovery reuses `config.FindGitRoot`. Preconditions fast-fail: `$TMUX` set (via `tmux.OriginalTMUX`, since `internal/tmux` init strips `$TMUX` from the process env) then `wt` on PATH; local `exitCodeError` wrapper maps 2 (precondition) / 3 (subprocess) without touching `main.execute()`. `exec.CommandContext` timeouts: wt 30s, tmux 10s. Single-quote escaping helper (`escapeSingleQuotes`) keeps `--cmd` values safe in the tmux shell string. `cmd/rk/context.go` CLI Commands section lists `rk riff` under **Workflow**. 13 new tests (8 for `parseWorktreePath`, 6 for `escapeSingleQuotes`, 5 for `resolveLauncher`, plus fabconfig coverage). (r1j6)

- **Update** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — docs: Fix rk riff doc comments and typos from Copilot review (#149)
- **Creation** [rk-riff](/run-kit/rk-riff.md) — refactor: Add rk riff command to unify worktree + tmux + Claude Code launcher (#146)

Preconditions: `$TMUX` set + `wt` on PATH (exit 2). SSE hub gains `event: metrics` event type broadcast to all clients every tick (server-independent, not deduplicated). `Server` struct holds `*metrics.Collector`, created and started in `NewRouter()`. Frontend: `SessionProvider` listens for `metrics` SSE event, exposes `MetricsSnapshot | null` via context. - **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Optimistic sidebar window reorder — drag-drop window reorder in sidebar now uses `useOptimisticAction` with `swapWindowOrder` store action to swap window index values immediately on drop. API call fires in background; rollback reverses the swap on failure. Eliminates ~2.5s SSE poll wait. `swapWindowOrder(session, srcIndex, dstIndex)` added to Zustand window store. Unit tests for store swap + rollback, sidebar tests for optimistic drop + API failure rollback. (sl01)

Rollback: `restoreWindow` + `removeGhost`. Removed `onMoveWindowToSession` prop from `SidebarProps` — sidebar imports `moveWindowToSession` API directly. Drag data payload extended with `windowId` and `name`. Unit tests for optimistic lifecycle and rollback. (sl02)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Sidebar collapsible panels — `CollapsiblePanel` reusable component (header + chevron + `max-height` transition + localStorage persistence). `StatusPanel` refactored into `WindowPanel` wrapping content in CollapsiblePanel (`storageKey="runkit-panel-window"`). Both panels bottom-aligned in sidebar. Hostname removed from bottom bar. New `lib/sparkline.ts` (8-level braille mapping U+2800-U+28FF) and `lib/gauge.ts` (block gauge with green/yellow/red thresholds, byte formatting). `SessionProvider` extended with `metrics: MetricsSnapshot | null` from SSE `event: metrics`. (z63r)

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Sidebar window state Zustand** — Window optimistic state migrated from index-based `OptimisticContext` to Zustand store (`app/frontend/src/store/window-store.ts`). `zustand` added as production dependency in `app/frontend/package.json`. `WindowInfo` type gains `windowId: string` (non-optional); backend adds `#{window_id}` as first field in `ListWindows()` format string (7 fields total, field 0 = `WindowID`). `OptimisticContext` slimmed to session/server scope. `MergedWindow` moved to `window-store.ts`. Ghost reconciliation uses `snapshotWindowIds` set-difference. Frontend unit tests: `window-store.test.ts` added, `optimistic-context.test.tsx` stripped of window test cases. (x3yt)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — refactor: Sidebar Window State Zustand (#123)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: Session Name Inline Rename (#121)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: left panel windows sync without page refresh (#122)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Fix left panel window sync — introduced `onAlwaysSettled`/`onAlwaysRollback` callbacks to `useOptimisticAction` that fire regardless of mount state (for root-level context cleanup like `unmarkKilled`), while `onSettled`/`onRollback` remain behind `mountedRef` guard (safe for local component state). Kill handlers in `sidebar.tsx` and `use-dialog-state.ts` migrated to `onAlways*`. E2E test `sidebar-window-sync.spec.ts` rewritten to be self-contained per test with unique window names and Scenario 3 using `page.route()` to intercept the kill API and exercise the unmount-before-response path. (2a2k)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Session inline rename — double-click session name in sidebar to edit inline (mirrors window rename pattern). Enter/blur commits (non-empty, changed only), Escape cancels. Optimistic update via `markRenamed("session", ...)` with toast on error. Cross-cancel: starting a session edit cancels any active window edit and vice versa — only one inline edit active at a time. Dialog-based session rename in `app.tsx` unchanged (3mt2)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Sidebar window state Zustand — window optimistic state migrated from index-based `OptimisticContext` to a Zustand store (`app/frontend/src/store/window-store.ts`) keyed by immutable `windowId` (`@N`). Eliminates index-collision bugs from tmux window renumbering. `WindowInfo` gains `windowId: string`. Backend adds `#{window_id}` to tmux format string. `OptimisticContext` slimmed to session/server scope only. `MergedWindow` moved to `window-store.ts`. `sidebar.tsx`, `app.tsx`, `use-dialog-state.ts` updated to use Zustand store actions. Ghost reconciliation uses snapshot `windowId` set-difference instead of count-based heuristics. (x3yt)

Added `unbind C-b` and `bind-key C-s send-prefix`. Frontend keyboard shortcuts label updated from `Ctrl+B, ` to `Ctrl+S, `. `byobu.conf` (`C-a`) and `poweruser.conf` (`C-s`) unchanged. (d7s5)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Multi-file tmux config sourcing** — `~/.rk/tmux.d/` drop-in directory for user extensions. `configs/tmux/default.conf` appends `source-file -q ~/.rk/tmux.d/*.conf` (lexicographic order, `-q` silences empty/missing dir). `EnsureConfig()`, `ForceWriteConfig()`, and `rk init-conf` all create the directory idempotently. `EnsureConfig()` creates `tmux.d/` even when config already exists. `ReloadConfig()` unchanged — transitive sourcing picks up new drop-ins. - **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — Remove Caddyfile and all Caddy references

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — Add keyboard shortcuts overlay, enhance tmux config

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — Remove pnpm workspace — single frontend package doesn't need it
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — refactor: single-active-server tmux model with server switcher UI
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — Rename upgrade to update, improve Homebrew update flow
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: add PWA compliance for homescreen install and standalone display
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — Remove BACKEND_PORT/BACKEND_HOST indirection, delete prod.sh

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — docs: update memory for multi-server relay, config reload, and stderr capture
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Release pipeline + update command** — `release.sh` rewritten (fab-kit/tu style). CI generates `Formula/rk.rb` in `sahil87/homebrew-tap` from `.github/formula-template.rb` (sed placeholder substitution for version + SHA256s) via `BUILD_TOKEN` secret. Public repo — plain URL downloads, no auth token needed. `upgrade` subcommand renamed to `update` (alias: `upgrade`): checks latest via `brew info --json=v2 sahil87/tap/rk`, skips if up-to-date, shows version transition.
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Multi-server relay + config reload** — `RK_TMUX_CONF` resolved to absolute path at init (fixes CWD-dependent config loading). Relay and select-window endpoints accept `?server=` query param to route to runkit or default tmux server (fixes default-server sessions not connecting). `SelectWindowOnServer()` added. `ReloadConfig(server)` hot-reloads tmux config via `source-file`. New `POST /api/tmux/reload-config` endpoint + `reloadTmuxConfig(server)` client function + "Reload tmux config" command palette action (targets current session's server). `tmuxExec`/`tmuxExecDefault` capture stderr in error messages. `TerminalClient` accepts `server` prop. (0gjh)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Single-active-server model** — Replaced dual-server merge with single-server-at-a-time. Backend stateless: `?server=` on every request, defaults to `"default"`. Unified `tmuxExec`/`tmuxExecDefault` into `tmuxExecServer`. All tmux functions accept `server` param. `serverFromRequest()` validates names. New `ListServers()` (socket scan), `KillServer()`. SSE hub polls per-server. Removed `SessionInfo.Server`, `ProjectSession.Server`. New `GET/POST /api/servers`, `POST /api/servers/kill`. Frontend: `SessionProvider` manages server state (localStorage `runkit-server`), sidebar server dropdown, palette commands (Create/Kill/Switch server). `setServerGetter()` mechanism appends `?server=` to all API calls. (1335)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **UI polish, tmux config, keyboard shortcuts** — Embed restructured from `app/backend/frontend/` to `app/backend/build/` (package `build`, `//go:embed all:frontend`). Breadcrumb left-aligned (removed `justify-center`). `EnsureConfig()` auto-creates `~/.run-kit/tmux.conf` on serve startup. `-f` config flag scoped to `CreateSession`/`ReloadConfig` via `configArgs()`. Enhanced `internal/tmux/tmux.conf`: `escape-time 0`, `history-limit 50000`, `renumber-windows on`, `base-index 1`, explicit `prefix C-s`, pane splits (`prefix+|`/`prefix+-`), pane navigation (`S-F3`/`S-F4`), `F8` rename, `S-F7` copy-mode. Sidebar server dropdown gains `+ tmux server` action. Hostname in bottom bar (hidden on mobile). Aligned sidebar footer and bottom bar heights (`h-[48px]`). Server label changed to "tmux server:". Kill server handles socket teardown gracefully. New `GET /api/keybindings` endpoint — runs `tmux list-keys`, filters via whitelist map, returns `[{key, table, command, label}]`. New "Keyboard Shortcuts" command palette action opens modal showing curated bindings grouped by key table (prefix vs root). (9ldy)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Daemon lifecycle** — Replaced `supervisor.sh` (polling loop + `.restart-requested` signal file) with CLI-driven daemon management. New `internal/daemon/` package with `IsRunning`/`Start`/`Stop`/`Restart` helpers using `exec.CommandContext`. `run-kit serve` gains `-d` (start daemon, errors if running), `--restart` (idempotent stop+start), `--stop` (graceful C-c) flags — mutually exclusive. Daemon runs in dedicated tmux server `rk-daemon` (session `rk`, window `serve`), separate from agent `runkit` server. `run-kit update` auto-restarts daemon after successful `brew upgrade`. Justfile `up`/`down`/`restart` recipes updated. Constitution Self-Improvement Safety section rewritten. (hkm8)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **PWA compliance** — `vite-plugin-pwa` with autoUpdate, manifest from plugin config (standalone display, dark theme colors), Workbox service worker (precache static, NetworkOnly for API/WebSocket), iOS meta tags, theme-color sync. No backend changes. (j9a2)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Hostname in browser title** — `Server` struct gains `hostname` field (computed once via `os.Hostname()` in `NewRouter()`, empty string fallback). `/api/health` response extended to `{"status":"ok","hostname":"..."}`. New `getHealth()` API client function. `useBrowserTitle` hook sets `document.title` dynamically: `RunKit — {hostname}` on Dashboard, `{session}/{window} — {hostname}` on terminal pages. Hostname suffix omitted when empty. (uq0k)

- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: Add light theme support with system/light/dark modes
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Dedicated tmux server** — All run-kit sessions live on a named tmux server `runkit` (via `tmux -L runkit`). `internal/tmux` commands prefixed with `-L runkit` and optional `-f` from `RK_TMUX_CONF` env var. `ListSessions()` queries both runkit and default servers, returning `SessionInfo` with `Server` field. `ListWindows()` accepts server parameter. `CreateSession()` uses plain `tmux new-session` on runkit server (byobu dependency removed, `sync.OnceValue` detection deleted). `ProjectSession` type gains `Server` field. Relay attaches to runkit server. New `internal/tmux/tmux.conf` with dark-themed status bar and F2/F3/F4 keybindings. Frontend: `ProjectSession` type gains `server` field, sidebar shows `↗` marker for default-server sessions. (0gjh)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Light theme support** — ThemeProvider context (outermost, split pattern) with three modes (system/light/dark). CSS `data-theme` attribute on `<html>` switches color tokens via `globals.css` selectors. Blocking inline script in `index.html` for no-flicker init. xterm terminal theme updates live. Theme switcher in command palette. Provider tree: `ThemeProvider > ChromeProvider > SessionProvider > AppShell`. (eseg)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — feat: Replace byobu with dedicated tmux server (-L runkit)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — Rewrote for multi-server architecture — dedicated `runkit` tmux server replaces byobu integration. `ListSessions()` queries both runkit and default servers. `parseSessions()` extracted as testable function with server tagging. `CreateSession()` uses plain `tmux new-session` (byobu dependency removed). `ListWindows()` accepts server parameter. (0gjh)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: Replace byobu with dedicated tmux server (-L runkit)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: Inline tab rename on double-click in sidebar
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — fix: Theme context double-read, button type attr, favicon load order, add tests
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: Add light theme support with system/light/dark modes
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Sidebar external session marker — `ProjectSession` type gains `server` field (`"runkit"` or `"default"`). Session rows show `↗` marker for default-server sessions (`text-[10px] text-text-secondary/50`, `aria-label="external session"`). Runkit-server sessions have no marker. (0gjh)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Inline tab rename — double-click window name in sidebar to edit inline (Enter/blur commits, Escape cancels, empty input cancels). Local state in Sidebar, no new dependencies. Existing command palette rename unchanged (dcl9)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Light theme support — three-mode theme system (system/light/dark), `data-theme` attribute on `<html>`, CSS custom properties per theme, blocking init script for no-flicker, ThemeProvider context (split pattern), xterm live theme update, command palette theme switcher, `--color-bg-inset` token replaces hardcoded fixed-width bg (eseg)

## 2026-03-17
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Homebrew deployment system** — Cobra CLI with subcommands. `embed.FS` bundles frontend into single binary. `VERSION` file + ldflags injection. `scripts/build.sh` (frontend-first build), `scripts/release.sh` (semver bump + tag). GitHub Actions CI/CD cross-compiles 4 targets (darwin/linux x arm64/amd64) on `v*` tag push, creates GitHub Release with tarballs. SPA handler dual-mode: embedded FS (production) or filesystem (dev). (ukyz)

- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Default session name from folder — Create Session dialog derives name from path when name field is empty at submit time. Create button enabled when path is set (even without explicit name). Derived name collision checked with error display (qiza)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Fix xterm clipboard copy — `copyToClipboard` helper with `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` primary + `document.execCommand('copy')` fallback for non-secure HTTP contexts. Selection cleared via `.finally()`. Exported for testability (rpqx)

## 2026-03-14
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — feat: Relay session validation, 4004 handling, and memory updates
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — refactor: Rename RUN_KIT_* env vars to RK_*, overhaul supervisor
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — docs: Pane Map Enrichment (#41)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Relay session validation** — relay handler validates session/window exist before attaching PTY. Returns WebSocket close code `4004` for missing session or window (distinct from `4001` PTY failure). Frontend handles `4004` by navigating to `/` instead of reconnecting. Prevents infinite reconnect loops when navigating to a non-existent tmux session.
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — ~~**Byobu session creation**~~ — Superseded by dedicated tmux server (`260318-0gjh-dedicated-tmux-server`). `CreateSession()` now uses plain `tmux new-session` on the `runkit` server with its own config (`internal/tmux/tmux.conf`).
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Pane-map enrichment** — replaced per-session `.fab-status.yaml` + `.fab-runtime.yaml` file reading with single `fab-go pane-map --json --all-sessions` subprocess call. Per-window fab state (change, stage, agent state, idle duration) instead of per-session. Deleted `internal/fab/` package (4 files). `internal/sessions` simplified: removed `enrichSession()`, `hasFabKit()`, `runtimeCache sync.Map`. New `fetchPaneMap(repoRoot)` + map join. (3vlx)
- **Update** [architecture](/run-kit/architecture.md) — **Top bar & bottom bar refresh** — Top bar: hamburger icon (☰→✕ animation) replaces logo as toggle, `/` separator replaces `❯`, session/window names are dropdown triggers, session name max 7ch. Right section: logo (decorative) + "Run Kit" + green dot (no text) + toggle + ⌘K + >_ compose. Mobile: ⋯ + >_. Bottom bar: removed Cmd modifier and compose button, sizes increased to 36px/44px. `onOpenCompose` moved from BottomBarProps to TopBarProps. (9raw)
- **Update** [tmux-sessions](/run-kit/tmux-sessions.md) — feat: Relay session validation, 4004 handling, and memory updates
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — feat: Relay session validation, 4004 handling, and memory updates
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — refactor: Remove Top Bar Line 2 (#42)
- **Update** [ui-patterns](/run-kit/ui-patterns.md) — Top bar & bottom bar refresh — hamburger icon replaces logo as sidebar toggle (animates ☰→✕), `/` separator replaces `❯`, session/window names are dropdown triggers (max 7ch session name). Top bar right: logo (decorative) + "Run Kit" + green dot (no text) + toggle + ⌘K + >_ compose. Mobile right: ⋯ + >_. Bottom bar: removed Cmd modifier and compose button, button sizes increased to 36px desktop / 44px touch (9raw)
